```python
def is_token_response(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a structurally valid token response.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenResponseValidation"></a>

## TokenResponseValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenResponseValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`parse_token_response`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.parse_token_response"></a>

#### parse\_token\_response

```python
def parse_token_response(value: object) -> TokenResponseValidation
```

Parse and validate a token-endpoint response body. (§23.5, R-23.8-b)

Confirms the REQUIRED ``access_token``/``token_type`` are present and that ``token_type``
is ``Bearer`` (case-insensitive, per RFC 6749) since MCP presents the token via the
``Bearer`` scheme (R-23.8-b). The presence of a ``refresh_token`` is left to the caller's
discretion-aware handling — never assumed (R-23.9-d).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.has_no_refresh_token"></a>

#### has\_no\_refresh\_token

```python
def has_no_refresh_token(token: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a token response did NOT issue a refresh token, so callers never
assume one was issued. (R-23.9-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.resource_parameter_for"></a>

#### resource\_parameter\_for

```python
def resource_parameter_for(canonical_resource_identifier: str) -> str
```

Return the ``resource`` parameter value for the MCP server — its canonical resource
identifier — that MUST be sent in BOTH the authorization and token requests, regardless of
whether the authorization server advertises ``resource`` support. (R-23.6-b, R-23.6-c,
R-23.6-d, R-23.6-e)

This is the identity of the canonical resource identifier; it is surfaced as a named
helper so call sites read intentionally and the "always send it" rule (R-23.6-e) is
explicit. The value SHOULD already be a canonical resource identifier (validate with S35's
``is_valid_canonical_resource_identifier``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenAudienceValidation"></a>

## TokenAudienceValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenAudienceValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_token_audience`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.validate_token_audience"></a>

#### validate\_token\_audience

```python
def validate_token_audience(
        token_audience,
        own_canonical_resource: str) -> TokenAudienceValidation
```

Validate, on the MCP server side, that a presented token was issued for THIS server as
the intended audience, rejecting any token whose audience is some other resource.
(R-23.6-f, R-23.6-g, R-23.6-h)

Compares the token's audience to this server's canonical resource identifier using S35's
``resource_identifiers_equal`` (accepting uppercase scheme/host for robustness,
R-23.1-p). A server MUST only accept tokens valid for its own resources and MUST NOT
accept (or forward) any other token (R-23.6-h). ``token_audience`` may be a single string
or a list of strings.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenSelectionResult"></a>

## TokenSelectionResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenSelectionResult()
```

Outcome of :func:`select_token_for_server`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.select_token_for_server"></a>

#### select\_token\_for\_server

```python
def select_token_for_server(*, server_issuer: str,
                            server_canonical_resource: str, token_issuer: str,
                            token_audience,
                            access_token: str) -> TokenSelectionResult
```

Select the access token a client may send to a given MCP server — strictly the one
issued by that server's authorization server for that server, and no other. (R-23.6-i)

Looks up the token recorded for ``server_issuer`` and confirms its audience is the
server's ``server_canonical_resource``. When no matching token exists, returns an error so
the client sends NOTHING rather than a wrong-audience token — a client MUST NOT send any
token other than one issued for that server (R-23.6-i).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.build_bearer_authorization_header"></a>

#### build\_bearer\_authorization\_header

```python
def build_bearer_authorization_header(access_token: str) -> str
```

Build the ``Authorization: Bearer <access-token>`` request header value a client MUST

send on every request to the MCP server. (R-23.8-a, R-23.8-b)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: When ``access_token`` is empty.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.extract_bearer_token"></a>

#### extract\_bearer\_token

```python
def extract_bearer_token(header_value: str | None) -> str | None
```

Extract the bearer token from an ``Authorization`` header value, or ``None`` when the
header is absent or does not use the ``Bearer`` scheme. (R-23.8-b)

The scheme match is case-insensitive per RFC 7235.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.url_contains_access_token_in_query"></a>

#### url\_contains\_access\_token\_in\_query

```python
def url_contains_access_token_in_query(request_url: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a URL carries an ``access_token`` in its query string, which a
client MUST NOT do. (R-23.8-c)

Use to assert that a request URL does not smuggle the token in the query string; the token
belongs only in the ``Authorization`` header (R-23.8-b).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenValidationContext"></a>

## TokenValidationContext Objects

```python
@dataclass
class TokenValidationContext()
```

A description of what an operation requires, against which the MCP server validates a
presented token on every request. (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenValidationContext.own_canonical_resource"></a>

#### own\_canonical\_resource

This server's canonical resource identifier (the expected audience). (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenValidationContext.resource_metadata"></a>

#### resource\_metadata

The protected-resource metadata URI for the ``WWW-Authenticate`` challenge.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.TokenValidationContext.required_scopes"></a>

#### required\_scopes

The scopes this specific operation requires; empty when none. (R-23.8-d, R-23.8-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.PresentedToken"></a>

## PresentedToken Objects

```python
@dataclass
class PresentedToken()
```

The validated facts about a presented token, supplied by signature/introspection.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.PresentedToken.active"></a>

#### active

Whether the signature or introspection result is valid. (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.PresentedToken.expired"></a>

#### expired

Whether the token is unexpired. (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.PresentedToken.audience"></a>

#### audience

The token's audience claim. (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.PresentedToken.scopes"></a>

#### scopes

The scopes the token grants. (R-23.8-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.AccessTokenValidation"></a>

## AccessTokenValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AccessTokenValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_access_token_request`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.AccessTokenValidation.challenge"></a>

#### challenge

On failure, the ``401``/``403`` challenge to return.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow.validate_access_token_request"></a>

#### validate\_access\_token\_request

```python
def validate_access_token_request(
        token: PresentedToken | None,
        context: TokenValidationContext) -> AccessTokenValidation
```

Validate a presented access token on the MCP server side, on EVERY request, yielding a
``401``/``403`` challenge on failure. (R-23.8-a, R-23.8-d, R-23.8-e, R-23.8-f)

The server treats each request independently and revalidates the token each time
(R-23.8-a). The checks, in order:
  - missing / inactive / expired token → ``401 Unauthorized`` (R-23.8-e);
  - wrong audience → ``401 Unauthorized`` (the token was not issued for this server;
    R-23.6-f/g, R-23.8-d/e);
  - valid token lacking a required scope → ``403 Forbidden`` with an ``insufficient_scope``
    challenge (R-23.8-f).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization\_registration

S37 — Authorization III: registration mechanisms, scopes & security (§23.11–§23.19).

S35 (:mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization`) established the authorization model and the
protected-resource / authorization-server metadata discovery; S36
(:mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_flow`) built the authorization-code-with-PKCE flow, token
exchange, audience binding, and the registration *data types* (CIMD / DCR schemas, the
``ClientIdMechanism`` enum, scope resolution helpers, the PKCE and ``iss`` validators). This
module completes the authorization surface by adding the parts S37 owns on top of that
foundation, WITHOUT re-implementing the PKCE/token flow:

  - **metadata-driven mechanism selection** — choosing the first applicable ``client_id``
    mechanism after inspecting the *validated authorization-server metadata*, gating CIMD on
    ``client_id_metadata_document_supported`` and DCR on ``registration_endpoint``
    (R-23.11-a – R-23.11-e);
  - **CIMD client-side hosting predicates** — the ``https``-with-path ``client_id`` URL rule
    and the document-identity rule, reusing S36's ``is_valid_cimd_client_id_url`` /
    ``validate_client_id_metadata_document`` and adding an AS-side cache (HTTP-cache-header
    aware) and host-domain trust policy (R-23.12-a – R-23.12-l);
  - **``application_type`` selection & DCR retry** — reusing S36's ``application_type_for`` /
    ``handle_dynamic_client_registration_response``, adding a loopback-aware native/web
    classifier and a bounded retry that adjusts the ``application_type``
    (R-23.15-a – R-23.15-f);
  - **credential binding to the issuer** — the issuer-keyed re-registration decision with the
    CIMD exemption and the SHOULD-surface-error behaviour, by exact string comparison
    (R-23.16-a – R-23.16-g);
  - **discovery robustness** — predicate wrappers over S35's
    ``protected_resource_well_known_uris`` / ``authorization_server_well_known_uris``, the
    ``authorization_servers`` requirement, and per-AS registration-state separation
    (R-23.17-a – R-23.17-i);
  - **scope selection & the step-up authorization flow** — the least-privilege selection
    (reusing S36's ``resolve_authorization_scope``), the scope *union* that never drops
    already-granted scopes, the bounded retry, and the per-resource-and-operation upgrade
    tracker (R-23.18-a – R-23.18-r, R-23.1-ae – R-23.1-ag);
  - **authorization security requirements** — consolidated predicates for audience binding,
    exact issuer validation, PKCE-mandatory, ``state``, token confidentiality, and
    refresh-token handling, delegating the mechanics to S36 (R-23.19-a – R-23.19-u).

Mirrors the TS SDK's ``protocol/authorization-registration.ts``. Builds on S35/S36 symbols,
none of which are redefined here.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CLIENT_ID_METADATA_DOCUMENT_SUPPORTED_FIELD"></a>

#### CLIENT\_ID\_METADATA\_DOCUMENT\_SUPPORTED\_FIELD

The authorization-server-metadata flag that gates the CIMD mechanism. When ``True``, the
AS supports Client ID Metadata Documents. (R-23.11-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.RegistrationMechanismSelection"></a>

## RegistrationMechanismSelection Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RegistrationMechanismSelection()
```

The mechanism chosen by :func:`select_registration_mechanism`, with the reason it
applied.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.RegistrationMechanismSelection.mechanism"></a>

#### mechanism

The selected mechanism, or ``"prompt"`` when none applies. (R-23.11-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.RegistrationMechanismSelection.reason"></a>

#### reason

Human-readable explanation of why this mechanism was selected.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.select_registration_mechanism"></a>

#### select\_registration\_mechanism

```python
def select_registration_mechanism(
        *,
        authorization_server_metadata: dict,
        has_pre_registered_credentials: bool = False,
        supported_mechanisms=None) -> RegistrationMechanismSelection
```

Select the ``client_id`` mechanism from the VALIDATED authorization-server metadata and
the client's credential state, applying the §23.11 priority order and the metadata gates.
(R-23.11-a, R-23.11-b, R-23.11-c, R-23.11-d, R-23.11-e)

The order, using the first that applies:
  1. pre-registration — when the client already holds credentials for this AS;
  2. CIMD — only when the AS metadata sets ``client_id_metadata_document_supported: true``
     (R-23.11-d) AND the client supports it;
  3. DCR — only when the AS metadata advertises a ``registration_endpoint`` (R-23.11-e) AND
     the client supports it;
  4. ``prompt`` — otherwise prompt the user for client information.

The function inspects the metadata before deciding (R-23.11-c) and never returns
``cimd``/``dcr`` when the corresponding gate is closed (R-23.11-d, R-23.11-e), so a caller
acting on the result will not attempt a mechanism the AS does not support. This complements
S36's ``select_client_id_mechanism``, which ranks a static capability set; here the live
metadata flags are the deciding input.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.may_attempt_cimd"></a>

#### may\_attempt\_cimd

```python
def may_attempt_cimd(metadata: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY attempt CIMD against this authorization server — i.e.
the metadata sets ``client_id_metadata_document_supported: true``. A client MUST NOT
attempt CIMD otherwise. (R-23.11-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.may_attempt_dcr"></a>

#### may\_attempt\_dcr

```python
def may_attempt_dcr(metadata: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY attempt Dynamic Client Registration against this
authorization server — i.e. the metadata advertises a ``registration_endpoint``. A client
MUST NOT attempt DCR otherwise. (R-23.11-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.PRIVATE_KEY_JWT_AUTH_METHOD"></a>

#### PRIVATE\_KEY\_JWT\_AUTH\_METHOD

The ``private_key_jwt`` token-endpoint authentication method a CIMD client MAY use.
(R-23.12-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.cimd_is_preferred_path"></a>

#### cimd\_is\_preferred\_path

```python
def cimd_is_preferred_path(client_supports_cimd: bool,
                           server_supports_cimd: bool) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when both a client and an authorization server should prefer CIMD as the
registration path — both SHOULD support the mechanism. (R-23.12-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.is_cimd_client_id_hosting_valid"></a>

#### is\_cimd\_client\_id\_hosting\_valid

```python
def is_cimd_client_id_hosting_valid(client_id_url: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``client_id_url`` satisfies the CIMD client-side hosting rules: it
is hosted at an ``https`` URL and the URL contains a path component. (R-23.12-b, R-23.12-c)

Delegates the ``https``+path check to S36's ``is_valid_cimd_client_id_url``; surfaced here
under the §23.12 atom so call sites read against this story's rule.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.cimd_supports_private_key_jwt"></a>

#### cimd\_supports\_private\_key\_jwt

```python
def cimd_supports_private_key_jwt(document: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a CIMD client MAY authenticate to the token endpoint with
``private_key_jwt``: the document declares that method and conveys an appropriate
``jwks``/``jwks_uri``. (R-23.12-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdCacheControl"></a>

## CimdCacheControl Objects

```python
@dataclass
class CimdCacheControl()
```

The HTTP caching directives an authorization server honours when caching a fetched CIMD
document. (R-23.12-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdCacheControl.max_age_seconds"></a>

#### max\_age\_seconds

``max-age`` in seconds from ``Cache-Control``, if any.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdCacheControl.no_store"></a>

#### no\_store

``True`` when ``Cache-Control: no-store`` (or ``no-cache``) forbids caching.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration._CachedCimdEntry"></a>

## \_CachedCimdEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass
class _CachedCimdEntry()
```

A cached CIMD document with its HTTP-cache freshness bookkeeping. (R-23.12-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration._CachedCimdEntry.expires_at_ms"></a>

#### expires\_at\_ms

Epoch milliseconds after which the cache entry is stale; ``None`` ⇒ never expires.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdDocumentCache"></a>

## CimdDocumentCache Objects

```python
class CimdDocumentCache()
```

An authorization-server-side cache for fetched CIMD documents that respects HTTP cache
headers and applies a host-domain trust policy. (R-23.12-k, R-23.12-l)

The AS SHOULD cache documents (R-23.12-k) and SHOULD apply CIMD security considerations
such as a trust policy over allowed client-hosting domains (R-23.12-l). This cache enforces
both: an optional ``trust_host`` predicate rejects documents hosted on disallowed domains
before they are stored, and a ``Cache-Control: no-store``/``no-cache`` directive (or a
non-positive ``max-age``) keeps a document out of the cache.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdDocumentCache.__init__"></a>

#### \_\_init\_\_

```python
def __init__(*,
             trust_host: Callable[[str], bool] | None = None,
             now: Callable[[], float] | None = None) -> None
```

**Arguments**:

- `trust_host`: OPTIONAL host-domain trust policy; a document whose ``client_id``
host fails this predicate is never cached or returned (R-23.12-l). Defaults to trusting
all hosts.
- `now`: OPTIONAL clock (epoch ms) for testing; defaults to a millisecond wall clock.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdDocumentCache.is_host_trusted"></a>

#### is\_host\_trusted

```python
def is_host_trusted(client_id_url: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the host of ``client_id_url`` is permitted by the trust policy.
(R-23.12-l)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdDocumentCache.store"></a>

#### store

```python
def store(client_id_url: str,
          document: dict,
          cache_control: CimdCacheControl | None = None) -> bool
```

Cache a fetched CIMD document keyed by its ``client_id`` URL, honouring HTTP cache
directives and the trust policy. Returns ``True`` when the document was stored, ``False``
when caching was declined (untrusted host, ``no-store``, or a non-positive ``max-age``).
(R-23.12-k, R-23.12-l)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CimdDocumentCache.get"></a>

#### get

```python
def get(client_id_url: str) -> dict | None
```

Return the cached document for ``client_id_url`` when present, trusted, and still
fresh; otherwise ``None``. A stale entry is evicted on access. (R-23.12-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.application_type_for_redirect_uris"></a>

#### application\_type\_for\_redirect\_uris

```python
def application_type_for_redirect_uris(redirect_uris) -> str
```

Classify a set of redirect URIs as native or web and return the ``application_type`` a
client SHOULD register, consistent with those URIs. (R-23.15-a, R-23.15-b, R-23.15-c)

Redirect URIs that all resolve to a loopback/localhost host indicate a native application
(desktop/mobile/CLI/locally hosted web app) → ``"native"``; otherwise a remote
browser-based application → ``"web"``. The classification follows S36's
``application_type_for`` with the loopback test that makes it consistent with the redirect
URIs (R-23.15-a).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.DcrRetryResult"></a>

## DcrRetryResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DcrRetryResult()
```

Outcome of :func:`register_with_retry`: the final result and the attempts made.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.DcrRetryResult.result"></a>

#### result

The final DCR result — success or the last failure.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.DcrRetryResult.attempts"></a>

#### attempts

The ``application_type`` of each attempt, in order, for diagnostics.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.register_with_retry"></a>

#### register\_with\_retry

```python
def register_with_retry(*,
                        initial_application_type: str,
                        attempt: Callable[[str], object],
                        max_attempts: int = 2) -> DcrRetryResult
```

Perform Dynamic Client Registration with bounded retry, surfacing a meaningful error and
retrying with an adjusted ``application_type`` when the AS rejects on a redirect-URI /
application-type constraint. (R-23.15-d, R-23.15-e, R-23.15-f)

A client MUST be prepared for OIDC redirect-URI rejection (R-23.15-d). Each attempt's
response is interpreted by S36's ``handle_dynamic_client_registration_response``; on a
retryable failure (e.g. a ``400`` redirect-URI/application-type constraint), the
``application_type`` is flipped (``native`` ↔ ``web``) for the next attempt (R-23.15-f), up
to ``max_attempts``. The returned ``result`` carries a human-readable ``reason`` on failure
for the client to surface (R-23.15-e). This never raises on an AS rejection — it returns the
structured failure.

``attempt`` performs one registration POST for the given ``application_type``, returning the
AS's HTTP status and parsed body as a mapping with ``"status"`` and ``"body"`` keys (or a
``(status, body)`` tuple). Injected so this is transport-agnostic.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentials"></a>

## IssuerBoundCredentials Objects

```python
@dataclass
class IssuerBoundCredentials()
```

Persisted client credentials bound to the issuing authorization server. (R-23.16-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentials.issuer"></a>

#### issuer

The issuing authorization server's ``issuer`` identifier; the storage key. (R-23.16-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentials.client_id"></a>

#### client\_id

The ``client_id`` issued by (or pre-registered with) that authorization server.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentials.client_secret"></a>

#### client\_secret

OPTIONAL ``client_secret`` for confidential clients.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentials.cimd"></a>

#### cimd

``True`` when these credentials are a Client ID Metadata Document: a portable,
self-hosted HTTPS-URL ``client_id`` with no per-issuer registration state, hence exempt
from issuer re-binding/re-registration. (R-23.16, CIMD exemption)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CredentialBindingAction"></a>

#### CredentialBindingAction

The action a client takes for a discovered issuer: ``"reuse"`` | ``"re-register"`` |
``"surface-error"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CredentialBindingDecision"></a>

## CredentialBindingDecision Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CredentialBindingDecision()
```

Outcome of :func:`decide_credential_binding`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CredentialBindingDecision.action"></a>

#### action

Whether to reuse the stored credentials, re-register, or surface an error.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.CredentialBindingDecision.reason"></a>

#### reason

Human-readable explanation, suitable for surfacing to a user/developer.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.issuers_match_exactly"></a>

#### issuers\_match\_exactly

```python
def issuers_match_exactly(a: str, b: str) -> bool
```

Compare two ``issuer`` identifiers by EXACT string match, the comparison mandated for
credential binding. No scheme/host case folding, default-port elision, trailing-slash, or
percent-encoding normalization is applied. (R-23.16-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.decide_credential_binding"></a>

#### decide\_credential\_binding

```python
def decide_credential_binding(
        *,
        stored: IssuerBoundCredentials | None,
        discovered_issuer: str,
        is_pre_registered: bool = False) -> CredentialBindingDecision
```

Decide whether a client may reuse stored credentials for the
protected-resource-indicated authorization server, must re-register, or should surface an
error. (R-23.16-c, R-23.16-d, R-23.16-e, R-23.16-f, R-23.16-g, CIMD exemption)

Decision logic, all issuer comparisons by exact string match (R-23.16-f):
  - CIMD credentials are exempt: a portable HTTPS-URL ``client_id`` has no per-issuer state,
    so ``reuse`` regardless of issuer (CIMD exemption);
  - no stored credentials, or the stored ``issuer`` matches the discovered ``issuer`` →
    ``reuse`` (no stored ⇒ ``re-register``);
  - stored ``issuer`` differs from the discovered ``issuer``:
      - DCR-obtained (no ``cimd``, not flagged pre-registered) → ``re-register`` with the
        new authorization server (R-23.16-d, R-23.16-e);
      - pre-registered (``is_pre_registered: True``) → ``surface-error``, because
        pre-registered credentials cannot be re-registered automatically and the client
        SHOULD surface an error rather than silently using mismatched credentials
        (R-23.16-c, R-23.16-g).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentialStore"></a>

## IssuerBoundCredentialStore Objects

```python
class IssuerBoundCredentialStore()
```

An issuer-keyed store for persisted, issuer-bound client credentials, keeping separate
registration state per authorization server. (R-23.16-a, R-23.16-b, R-23.17-d)

The storage key is the authorization server's ``issuer`` identifier (R-23.16-b);
:meth:`credentials_for` never returns another issuer's credentials, so a caller cannot reuse
credentials across authorization servers (R-23.16-c). Distinct from S36's
``DynamicClientRegistrationStore`` (DCR-specific) and S35's ``CredentialStore`` (runtime
tokens): this holds the persisted registration identity for ALL mechanisms (pre-registration
and DCR), flagged with the CIMD exemption.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentialStore.save"></a>

#### save

```python
def save(credentials: IssuerBoundCredentials) -> None
```

Persist ``credentials``, keyed by their ``issuer``. (R-23.16-a, R-23.16-b)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: When ``credentials.issuer`` is empty — the key is REQUIRED.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentialStore.credentials_for"></a>

#### credentials\_for

```python
def credentials_for(issuer: str) -> IssuerBoundCredentials | None
```

Return the credentials stored for ``issuer``, or ``None``. Never another issuer's.
(R-23.16-b, R-23.16-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentialStore.has"></a>

#### has

```python
def has(issuer: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when credentials are stored for ``issuer``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.IssuerBoundCredentialStore.decide_for"></a>

#### decide\_for

```python
def decide_for(discovered_issuer: str,
               is_pre_registered: bool = False) -> CredentialBindingDecision
```

Return the :class:`CredentialBindingDecision` for the credentials stored under
``discovered_issuer``, the convenience entry point combining lookup and
:func:`decide_credential_binding`. (R-23.16-c – R-23.16-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.protected_resource_metadata_uris"></a>

#### protected\_resource\_metadata\_uris

```python
def protected_resource_metadata_uris(
        *,
        resource_metadata_url: str | None = None,
        mcp_endpoint_url: str | None = None) -> list[str]
```

Resolve the ordered protected-resource-metadata URIs to try, honouring the
``WWW-Authenticate`` ``resource_metadata`` precedence. (R-23.17-a, R-23.17-b)

  - When the ``401`` carried a ``resource_metadata`` URL, that single URL MUST be used
    (R-23.17-a);
  - otherwise the well-known URIs are returned in order — path-prefixed first, then host
    root — via S35's ``protected_resource_well_known_uris`` (R-23.17-b).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.AuthorizationServersValidation"></a>

## AuthorizationServersValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AuthorizationServersValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`require_authorization_servers`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.require_authorization_servers"></a>

#### require\_authorization\_servers

```python
def require_authorization_servers(
        metadata: dict) -> AuthorizationServersValidation
```

Validate that protected-resource metadata carries the REQUIRED ``authorization_servers``
array of one or more issuer identifiers. (R-23.17-c)

A valid document MUST contain ``authorization_servers`` with at least one entry; when more
than one is listed, each is an independent authorization server the client selects among,
maintaining separate registration state per AS (R-23.17-d, enforced by
:class:`IssuerBoundCredentialStore`).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.authorization_server_metadata_uris"></a>

#### authorization\_server\_metadata\_uris

```python
def authorization_server_metadata_uris(issuer: str) -> list[str]
```

Return the ordered authorization-server-metadata well-known URIs to try for ``issuer``,
covering both OAuth 2.0 AS Metadata and OpenID Connect Discovery, for issuers with and
without a path component. (R-23.17-e, R-23.17-f, R-23.17-g)

A thin pass-through over S35's ``authorization_server_well_known_uris``, surfaced under the
§23.17 atoms; returns the three path-component URIs (OAuth insertion, OIDC insertion, OIDC
appending) for a path issuer and the two for a non-path issuer, in the mandated priority
order.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.DiscoveredIssuerValidation"></a>

## DiscoveredIssuerValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DiscoveredIssuerValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_discovered_issuer`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.validate_discovered_issuer"></a>

#### validate\_discovered\_issuer

```python
def validate_discovered_issuer(
        document_issuer: str,
        expected_issuer: str) -> DiscoveredIssuerValidation
```

Validate that a fetched authorization-server metadata document's ``issuer`` is IDENTICAL
to the issuer used to construct the well-known URL; if it differs the document MUST NOT be
used. (R-23.17-h, R-23.17-i)

Exact string comparison — the same mix-up defence S35's
``validate_authorization_server_metadata`` performs; this surfaces just the issuer-identity
check under the §23.17 atoms for callers that have already structurally validated the
document.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.parse_scope_set"></a>

#### parse\_scope\_set

```python
def parse_scope_set(scope: str | None) -> list[str]
```

Split a space-delimited scope string into a deduplicated, order-preserving list.
Empty/whitespace-only input yields ``[]``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.format_scope_set"></a>

#### format\_scope\_set

```python
def format_scope_set(scopes) -> str
```

Serialize a scope list back into a space-delimited string.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.select_initial_scopes"></a>

#### select\_initial\_scopes

```python
def select_initial_scopes(
        *,
        challenge=None,
        protected_resource: dict | None = None) -> str | None
```

Select the least-privilege scopes for the initial authorization handshake, applying the
§23.18 priority: the ``WWW-Authenticate`` challenge ``scope`` (treated as authoritative,
with no assumed relationship to ``scopes_supported``), else all of ``scopes_supported``,
else omit the ``scope`` parameter entirely. (R-23.18-a, R-23.18-b, R-23.18-c, R-23.18-d)

Delegates to S36's ``resolve_authorization_scope``, whose priority order is identical;
surfaced here under the §23.18 atoms. Returns ``None`` to signal the ``scope`` parameter is
omitted (R-23.18-d).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.union_step_up_scopes"></a>

#### union\_step\_up\_scopes

```python
def union_step_up_scopes(already_granted, challenged_scopes) -> list[str]
```

Compute the UNION of already-granted/already-requested scopes with the newly-challenged
scopes, the scope set a step-up re-authorization requests. (R-23.18-o, R-23.18-p, R-23.1-ae)

Order-preserving and deduplicating: every already-granted scope is retained (R-23.18-p —
never dropped) and the challenged scopes are appended. The result is the authoritative
requested-scope set for the re-authorization. Hierarchically redundant scopes are NOT
deduplicated semantically — the AS normalizes that during issuance (R-23.18-r).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpActor"></a>

#### StepUpActor

Who the client is acting for, governing whether a step-up flow is attempted: ``"user"``
(SHOULD attempt step-up; R-23.18-m) or ``"client_credentials"`` (MAY attempt or abort;
R-23.18-n).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.should_attempt_step_up"></a>

#### should\_attempt\_step\_up

```python
def should_attempt_step_up(actor: StepUpActor) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client SHOULD attempt the step-up flow for a scope-related error:
always for a user-acting client (R-23.18-m); for a ``client_credentials`` client it MAY
attempt or abort, so this returns ``False`` (the conservative default — the caller MAY
override). (R-23.18-l, R-23.18-m, R-23.18-n)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeKey"></a>

## ScopeUpgradeKey Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ScopeUpgradeKey()
```

A scope-upgrade attempt key: the resource-and-operation combination being upgraded.
(R-23.18-r)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeKey.resource"></a>

#### resource

The MCP server's canonical resource identifier.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeKey.operation"></a>

#### operation

The operation (e.g. the MCP method) being attempted.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpAction"></a>

#### StepUpAction

The next action a step-up driver should take: ``"retry"`` | ``"permanent-failure"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker"></a>

## ScopeUpgradeTracker Objects

```python
class ScopeUpgradeTracker()
```

Tracks bounded step-up retry attempts per resource-and-operation combination, so a client
retries no more than a few times and treats persistent failure as a permanent authorization
failure. (R-23.18-q, R-23.18-r, R-23.1-af, R-23.1-ag)

Each :class:`ScopeUpgradeKey` accumulates an attempt count; once the bound is reached,
:meth:`next_action` returns ``"permanent-failure"`` rather than ``"retry"``, implementing the
retry limit (R-23.18-q) and the per-resource-and-operation attempt tracking that avoids
repeated failures for the same combination (R-23.18-r, R-23.1-ag).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.__init__"></a>

#### \_\_init\_\_

```python
def __init__(max_attempts: int = 3) -> None
```

**Arguments**:

- `max_attempts`: The maximum number of step-up attempts per
resource-and-operation; MUST be a few at most. Defaults to ``3``. (R-23.18-q)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: When ``max_attempts`` is not a positive integer.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.max_attempts"></a>

#### max\_attempts

```python
@property
def max_attempts() -> int
```

The configured retry bound.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.attempts_for"></a>

#### attempts\_for

```python
def attempts_for(key: ScopeUpgradeKey) -> int
```

Return the number of step-up attempts recorded so far for ``key``. (R-23.1-ag)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.can_retry"></a>

#### can\_retry

```python
def can_retry(key: ScopeUpgradeKey) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when another step-up attempt is permitted for ``key`` (the bound has not
been reached). (R-23.18-q)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.record_attempt"></a>

#### record\_attempt

```python
def record_attempt(key: ScopeUpgradeKey) -> int
```

Record one step-up attempt for ``key`` and return the new attempt count. (R-23.1-ag)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.next_action"></a>

#### next\_action

```python
def next_action(key: ScopeUpgradeKey) -> StepUpAction
```

Record an attempt for ``key`` and return whether to ``"retry"`` or treat the failure as
a ``"permanent-failure"``, implementing the bounded retry. After the bound is reached,
persistent failure MUST be treated as a permanent authorization failure. (R-23.18-q,
R-23.1-af)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ScopeUpgradeTracker.reset"></a>

#### reset

```python
def reset(key: ScopeUpgradeKey) -> None
```

Clear the attempt count for ``key`` (e.g. after a successful retry).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpPlan"></a>

## StepUpPlan Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StepUpPlan()
```

A plan for one step-up re-authorization, from :func:`plan_step_up_authorization`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpPlan.proceed"></a>

#### proceed

Whether a step-up should be attempted at all (per the actor and retry bound).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpPlan.scopes"></a>

#### scopes

The UNION scope set to request on re-authorization, when ``proceed``. (R-23.18-o)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpPlan.scope"></a>

#### scope

The space-delimited ``scope`` parameter for the re-authorization request.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.StepUpPlan.reason"></a>

#### reason

When ``proceed`` is ``False``, why the step-up is not attempted.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.plan_step_up_authorization"></a>

#### plan\_step\_up\_authorization

```python
def plan_step_up_authorization(
        *,
        actor: StepUpActor,
        already_granted,
        challenge,
        key: ScopeUpgradeKey,
        tracker: ScopeUpgradeTracker,
        force_for_client_credentials: bool = False) -> StepUpPlan
```

Plan one step-up re-authorization end to end: decide whether to proceed (by actor and
remaining retries), compute the UNION scope set that never drops already-granted scopes, and
record the attempt against the bound. (R-23.18-l, R-23.18-m, R-23.18-n, R-23.18-o, R-23.18-p,
R-23.18-q, R-23.18-r, R-23.1-ae, R-23.1-af, R-23.1-ag)

Proceeds when (a) the actor SHOULD/elects to step up — a user-acting client, or a
``client_credentials`` client with ``force_for_client_credentials`` — AND (b) the tracker
still permits a retry for the ``key``. When it proceeds it records the attempt (R-23.1-ag)
and returns the unioned ``scopes``/``scope`` for a fresh authorization-code+PKCE flow (built
with S36's ``build_authorization_request``). When the retry bound is exhausted it returns
``proceed=False`` so the caller treats the failure as permanent (R-23.18-q).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ResourceBindingValidation"></a>

## ResourceBindingValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResourceBindingValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`check_resource_parameter_binding`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.check_resource_parameter_binding"></a>

#### check\_resource\_parameter\_binding

```python
def check_resource_parameter_binding(
        *,
        authorization_request_resource: str | None = None,
        token_request_resource: str | None = None,
        canonical_resource: str) -> ResourceBindingValidation
```

Validate the audience-binding requirement: the SAME ``resource`` parameter, identifying
the MCP server by its canonical URI, MUST be present in BOTH the authorization request and
the token request, regardless of advertised AS support. (R-23.19-a)

A client MUST implement Resource Indicators by always sending ``resource`` in both legs
(R-23.19-a). This confirms both are present and byte-identical to ``canonical_resource``;
S36's ``assert_resource_matches_step2`` performs the equivalent Step-2/Step-4 invariant, and
is reused by callers that already hold the request objects.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.may_forward_token_to_server"></a>

#### may\_forward\_token\_to\_server

```python
def may_forward_token_to_server(token_issuer: str, server_issuer: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY send the access token it holds for ``token_issuer`` to
the MCP server whose authorization server is ``server_issuer`` — strictly only when the
issuers match exactly. A client MUST NOT send a token to an MCP server other than one issued
by that server's authorization server. (R-23.19-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.ExactIssuerValidation"></a>

## ExactIssuerValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ExactIssuerValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_exact_issuer`, the §23.19 mix-up-defence check.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.validate_exact_issuer"></a>

#### validate\_exact\_issuer

```python
def validate_exact_issuer(
        *,
        iss: str | None = None,
        recorded_issuer: str,
        iss_parameter_supported: bool | None = None) -> ExactIssuerValidation
```

Validate the authorization response's ``iss`` against the recorded issuer by exact string
comparison — the mix-up defence a client MUST perform BEFORE transmitting the authorization
code, including the ``authorization_response_iss_parameter_supported`` reject rule.
(R-23.19-e, R-23.19-f, R-23.19-g, R-23.19-h)

Delegates to S36's ``validate_issuer`` (the §23.7 decision table); surfaced here under the
§23.19 security atoms. The recorded issuer MUST have been captured before redirect
(R-23.19-e) and stored with the PKCE verifier and ``state`` in the same per-request record
(R-23.19-j, see :func:`same_request_record`). On failure the caller MUST NOT redeem the code
or display the response's ``error``/details (R-23.19-i, S36's ``safe_authorization_error``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord"></a>

## SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord Objects

```python
@dataclass
class SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord()
```

The per-request record that MUST hold the recorded issuer, PKCE code verifier, and
``state`` together. (R-23.19-e, R-23.19-j, R-23.19-k, R-23.19-l)

Storing all three in one record is what lets the redirect handler validate ``iss`` (against
``recorded_issuer``), ``state``, and PKCE coherently. This mirrors S36's
``AuthorizationFlowRecord``; it is restated here as the §23.19 security invariant the
consolidated check :func:`same_request_record` asserts.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord.recorded_issuer"></a>

#### recorded\_issuer

The validated ``issuer``, recorded BEFORE redirect. (R-23.19-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord.code_verifier"></a>

#### code\_verifier

The PKCE ``code_verifier``. (R-23.19-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord.state"></a>

#### state

The unpredictable anti-CSRF ``state``. (R-23.19-l)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.RequestRecordValidation"></a>

## RequestRecordValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestRecordValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`same_request_record`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.same_request_record"></a>

#### same\_request\_record

```python
def same_request_record(record) -> RequestRecordValidation
```

Assert that the recorded issuer, PKCE code verifier, and ``state`` are all present in the
same per-request record, the §23.19 storage invariant. (R-23.19-j)

All three MUST be co-located so the redirect can be validated coherently; an empty field
means the record is incomplete and the flow MUST NOT proceed. ``record`` may be a
:class:`SecureAuthorizationRequestRecord`, any object with those attributes, or a dict.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.is_confidential_token"></a>

#### is\_confidential\_token

```python
def is_confidential_token() -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a value MUST NOT be logged or forwarded because it is an access or
refresh token — the token-confidentiality guard. Always ``True``: access and refresh tokens
MUST NOT be logged and MUST NOT be forwarded to third parties. (R-23.19-m, R-23.19-n)

Use to gate logging/forwarding sinks: ``if is_confidential_token(): skip_logging()``. It
takes no token argument by design — the rule is unconditional, so it never incentivizes
passing a token where it might be captured.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.redact_token"></a>

#### redact\_token

```python
def redact_token() -> str
```

Return a redacted placeholder for a token so diagnostics never carry the secret itself,
enforcing token confidentiality at log/forward sinks. (R-23.19-m, R-23.19-n, R-23.19-o)

Access and refresh tokens MUST NOT be logged or forwarded; when a diagnostic must reference
"the token", use this redaction instead of the value. Returns a fixed marker regardless of
input, so the secret is never embedded.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.BearerHeaderValidation"></a>

## BearerHeaderValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BearerHeaderValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`check_bearer_header_only`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.check_bearer_header_only"></a>

#### check\_bearer\_header\_only

```python
def check_bearer_header_only(
        *, request_url: str,
        has_authorization_header: bool) -> BearerHeaderValidation
```

Validate that the access token is presented ONLY in the ``Authorization: Bearer`` request
header and NEVER in the URI query string. (R-23.19-p)

Reuses S36's ``url_contains_access_token_in_query`` to reject a request URL that smuggles
``access_token`` in the query, and requires an ``Authorization`` header to be present (the
token's only permitted location).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.grant_types_with_refresh"></a>

#### grant\_types\_with\_refresh

```python
def grant_types_with_refresh(grant_types) -> list[str]
```

Return the ``grant_types`` a client wanting refresh tokens SHOULD register: the given
grant types plus ``refresh_token`` (deduplicated). (R-23.19-r)

A client that wants refresh tokens SHOULD include ``refresh_token`` in its ``grant_types``
client metadata; this ensures it is present without duplicating it.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.with_offline_access_if_advertised"></a>

#### with\_offline\_access\_if\_advertised

```python
def with_offline_access_if_advertised(
        scopes, authorization_server_meta: dict) -> list[str]
```

Add ``offline_access`` to a ``scope`` list when, and only when, the authorization-server
metadata advertises it in ``scopes_supported``, for a client that wants a refresh token.
(R-23.19-s)

A client MAY add ``offline_access`` only when the AS lists it; when it is not advertised the
scope is returned unchanged. The result is deduplicated. Mirrors S36's
``with_offline_access_scope`` behaviour under the §23.19 refresh atom; provided as a
list-shaped helper for the scope-list call sites in this story.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.refresh_token_is_never_assumed"></a>

#### refresh\_token\_is\_never\_assumed

```python
def refresh_token_is_never_assumed() -> bool
```

Return ``True`` — a client MUST NOT assume a refresh token will be issued; the
authorization server retains discretion. (R-23.19-t)

A guard for control flow: treat the refresh token as optional and handle its absence. Pair
with S36's ``has_no_refresh_token`` to detect a token response that did not issue one.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.OfflineAccessOmissionValidation"></a>

## OfflineAccessOmissionValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class OfflineAccessOmissionValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`server_scopes_omit_offline_access`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.authorization_registration.server_scopes_omit_offline_access"></a>

#### server\_scopes\_omit\_offline\_access

```python
def server_scopes_omit_offline_access(
        *,
        challenge_scope: str | None = None,
        scopes_supported=None) -> OfflineAccessOmissionValidation
```

Validate that a server (protected resource) does NOT include ``offline_access`` in its
``WWW-Authenticate`` ``scope`` or in its ``scopes_supported``, as a server SHOULD ensure —
refresh tokens are not a resource requirement. (R-23.19-u)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.caching

Response caching hints (§13).

The ``CacheableResult`` fields (``ttlMs`` / ``cacheScope``) and runtime utilities for
validating hints, computing freshness from the client's local clock, resolving the
``cacheScope`` privacy fallback, detecting inconsistent scope across pages, and the
method → invalidation-notification map, plus a minimal in-memory ``ResponseCache``.

Caching hints are purely advisory: they never alter a result's meaning, never function
as access control, and clients MAY ignore them. (R-13.4-f, R-13.3-d, R-13.3-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.CacheScope"></a>

#### CacheScope

The two sharing-scope values as a field type (the analogue of the TS ``CacheScopeSchema``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.CACHE_SCOPES"></a>

#### CACHE\_SCOPES

The two sharing-scope values for a cached result. (§13.3) Unknown/absent → "private".

``"public"`` — any client or shared intermediary MAY reuse the stored copy for any
user, subject to the freshness interval. (R-13.3-a)

``"private"`` — may be stored and reused only within the single authorization context
that made the request; a shared intermediary MUST NOT serve a stored ``"private"``
copy to a different user. (R-13.3-b, R-13.3-c)

Any unrecognized or absent value MUST be treated as ``"private"``. (R-13.1-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.is_valid_cache_scope"></a>

#### is\_valid\_cache\_scope

```python
def is_valid_cache_scope(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` only when ``value`` is exactly the case-sensitive string
``"public"`` or ``"private"``. (§13.3, R-13.1-d)

This is the Python analogue of the TS ``CacheScopeSchema`` zod enum: it is strict
(``"PUBLIC"``, ``"shared"``, ``None``, etc. all fail). Use :func:`resolve_cache_scope`
when you want the privacy fallback instead of a boolean check.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.is_cache_hint_valid"></a>

#### is\_cache\_hint\_valid

```python
def is_cache_hint_valid(ttl_ms: object, cache_scope: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when BOTH hints are present and valid: non-negative integer
``ttlMs`` and ``cacheScope`` exactly ``"public"`` or ``"private"``. (R-13.1-a/-b/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.has_both_or_neither_cache_hints"></a>

#### has\_both\_or\_neither\_cache\_hints

```python
def has_both_or_neither_cache_hints(result: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``result`` carries BOTH hint fields or NEITHER. A server MUST
NOT emit exactly one without the other. (R-13.1-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.CacheableResult"></a>

## CacheableResult Objects

```python
class CacheableResult(McpModel)
```

A result augmented with the two REQUIRED caching-hint fields (§13.1) — the Python
analogue of the TS ``CacheableResultSchema``.

* REQUIRED string ``resultType`` (the §3.6 / S04 base discriminator).
* REQUIRED non-negative integer ``ttlMs`` (``0`` allowed; negatives / non-integers /
  ``bool`` rejected — ``StrictInt`` accepts only a real ``int``). (R-13.1-a, R-13.2-a)
* REQUIRED ``cacheScope`` exactly ``"public"`` or ``"private"``. (R-13.1-d)
* OPTIONAL object ``_meta``.

Both hint fields MUST be present together (R-13.1-g); method-specific payload members
(``tools`` / ``contents`` / ``nextCursor`` …) pass through (forward-compatible).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.is_valid_cacheable_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_cacheable\_result

```python
def is_valid_cacheable_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``CacheableResult`` wire object. (§13.1)

The base ``Result`` shape augmented with the two REQUIRED caching-hint fields, both of
which MUST appear and be valid (see :class:`CacheableResult`). (R-13.1-a/-d/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.resolve_cache_scope"></a>

#### resolve\_cache\_scope

```python
def resolve_cache_scope(scope: object) -> str
```

Return ``"public"``/``"private"``, applying the privacy fallback for any
unrecognized or absent value. (R-13.1-e, R-13.3-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.is_fresh"></a>

#### is\_fresh

```python
def is_fresh(ttl_ms: int, received_at: float, now: float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the result is still within its freshness window:
``ttlMs > 0`` AND ``now < received_at + ttlMs``. Uses only the client's local clock.
(R-13.2-e/-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.expires_at"></a>

#### expires\_at

```python
def expires_at(ttl_ms: int, received_at: float) -> float
```

Return the absolute timestamp after which the result is stale. (R-13.2-e/-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.has_consistent_cache_scope"></a>

#### has\_consistent\_cache\_scope

```python
def has_consistent_cache_scope(scopes: list[str]) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when all ``cacheScope`` values across a list's pages are identical
(no mixing of public/private). (R-13.5-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.effective_cache_scope"></a>

#### effective\_cache\_scope

```python
def effective_cache_scope(scopes: list[str]) -> str
```

Return the scope to apply across a multi-page list; ``"private"`` if inconsistent.
(R-13.5-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.METHOD_TO_NOTIFICATION_MAP"></a>

#### METHOD\_TO\_NOTIFICATION\_MAP

Maps each cacheable method to the notification that invalidates its cached result. (§13.5)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.methods_for_notification"></a>

#### methods\_for\_notification

```python
def methods_for_notification(notification: str) -> list[str]
```

Return the method names whose cached results should be invalidated on
``notification``. (R-13.5-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.CacheGetResult"></a>

## CacheGetResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CacheGetResult()
```

Outcome of :meth:`ResponseCache.get`: ``hit`` plus ``value``/``cache_scope`` on a hit.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.ResponseCache"></a>

## ResponseCache Objects

```python
class ResponseCache()
```

A minimal in-memory response cache wired to :func:`is_fresh`,
:func:`resolve_cache_scope`, and the method→notification invalidation map. (§13)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.ResponseCache.set"></a>

#### set

```python
def set(key: str, value: dict, received_at: float) -> None
```

Store ``value`` under ``key``; skipped when a hint is missing or invalid. A
``ttlMs == 0`` entry is stored but never served fresh. (R-13.1-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.ResponseCache.get"></a>

#### get

```python
def get(key: str, now: float) -> CacheGetResult
```

Return the entry for ``key`` if fresh at ``now``; otherwise miss + evict the stale
entry. (R-13.2-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.ResponseCache.invalidate_by_notification"></a>

#### invalidate\_by\_notification

```python
def invalidate_by_notification(notification: str) -> None
```

Evict all entries (incl. paginated cursor pages) for every method mapped to
``notification``. (R-13.5-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.ResponseCache.size"></a>

#### size

```python
@property
def size() -> int
```

Number of entries currently stored (may include ``ttlMs == 0`` entries).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.CACHEABLE_METHODS"></a>

#### CACHEABLE\_METHODS

The five methods whose results carry caching hints. (§13.4)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.caching.is_cacheable_method"></a>

#### is\_cacheable\_method

```python
def is_cacheable_method(method: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``method`` is one of the five methods that carry caching hints.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities

Per-request capability negotiation model (§1.5, §2.2.2).

Capabilities are declared per request, not once per connection. A client attaches its
declared capabilities to each request; a server MUST NOT infer a capability from any
prior request, connection, or stream (R-2.2.2-a, AC-01.14).

An endpoint MUST NOT exercise a feature the peer has not declared. If processing a
request requires an undeclared capability, the server MUST reject it with the dedicated
missing-capability error (R-2.2.2-b/-c). The numeric wire code lives in
:mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.meta` / :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.errors` (``-32003``); this module models
the per-request assertion with a symbolic-coded exception.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities.MissingCapabilityError"></a>

## MissingCapabilityError Objects

```python
class MissingCapabilityError(Exception)
```

Raised when a request requires a capability the peer did not declare for it.

(R-2.2.2-c, AC-01.15) Carries a symbolic ``code`` and the offending ``capability``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities.MissingCapabilityError.code"></a>

#### code

Symbolic code; the numeric wire value is ``-32003`` (see protocol.errors).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities.assert_capability"></a>

#### assert\_capability

```python
def assert_capability(declared_capabilities: Set[str], required: str) -> None
```

Assert that ``required`` has been declared by the peer for the current request.

Stateless by design: the caller supplies the capabilities declared on *this* request,
never accumulated state, enforcing the per-request rule (R-2.2.2-a, AC-01.14).

**Raises**:

- `MissingCapabilityError`: when ``required`` is not in ``declared_capabilities``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capabilities.has_capability"></a>

#### has\_capability

```python
def has_capability(declared_capabilities: Set[str], required: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``required`` has been declared for the current request.

Prefer :func:`assert_capability` in enforcement code; use this for conditional logic.
(R-2.2.2-b, AC-01.12, AC-01.13)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.capability\_negotiation

Capability Negotiation: client & server capabilities (§6.1–§6.4).

The capability layer: the declaration shapes and the per-request, stateless negotiation
rules that gate every optional feature. Because MCP is stateless, a feature is usable
only when BOTH peers declare the governing capability/sub-flag.

Provides presence-means-supported predicates (with the ``elicitation.form`` implicit
baseline and the boolean ``listChanged``/``subscribe`` sub-flags), the method/
notification gating maps, the ``-32003`` missing-capability gate + HTTP status mapping,
the sub-flag usage rules, and the graceful-degradation decision.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.ClientCapabilities"></a>

## ClientCapabilities Objects

```python
class ClientCapabilities(McpModel)
```

The optional client behaviors, supplied on every request (§6.2). An empty ``{}`` is
valid; all fields are OPTIONAL; ``roots`` / ``sampling`` are Deprecated.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.ServerCapabilities"></a>

## ServerCapabilities Objects

```python
class ServerCapabilities(McpModel)
```

The optional server behaviors, learned from ``server/discover`` (§6.3). An empty ``{}``
is valid; all fields are OPTIONAL; ``logging`` is Deprecated.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.is_valid_client_capabilities"></a>

#### is\_valid\_client\_capabilities

```python
def is_valid_client_capabilities(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a structurally valid ``ClientCapabilities`` object (§6.2, R-6.2-s).

An empty ``{}`` is valid (declares no optional behaviors), all fields are OPTIONAL, and
unknown top-level keys are tolerated (forward-compatible §2.6). When present, each known
field MUST have the right shape (see :class:`ClientCapabilities`).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.is_valid_server_capabilities"></a>

#### is\_valid\_server\_capabilities

```python
def is_valid_server_capabilities(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a structurally valid ``ServerCapabilities`` object (§6.3, R-6.3-s).

An empty ``{}`` is valid, all fields are OPTIONAL, and unknown top-level keys are
tolerated. When present, each known field MUST have the right shape (see
:class:`ServerCapabilities`).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.client_declares"></a>

#### client\_declares

```python
def client_declares(caps: dict, capability: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the client's capabilities declare ``capability`` (§6.1).

Presence of an object means supported. ``elicitation.form`` is the implicit baseline
(supported whenever ``elicitation`` is present); ``elicitation.url`` /
``sampling.context`` / ``sampling.tools`` require their own sub-flag object.
(R-6.2-e/-f/-g/-n/-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.server_declares"></a>

#### server\_declares

```python
def server_declares(caps: dict, capability: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server's capabilities declare ``capability`` (§6.2).

Object capabilities are declared by presence; boolean sub-flags
(``listChanged``/``subscribe``) only when explicitly ``True``. (R-6.3-h/-l/-o)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.server_method_required_capability"></a>

#### server\_method\_required\_capability

```python
def server_method_required_capability(method: str) -> str | None
```

The capability that gates ``method``, or ``None`` for an ungated (core) method.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_client_invoke"></a>

#### may\_client\_invoke

```python
def may_client_invoke(method: str, server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY invoke ``method`` given the server's capabilities.

Core (ungated) methods are always invocable; a gated method needs the governing
capability declared. (R-6.3-e, R-6.4-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.notification_required_capability"></a>

#### notification\_required\_capability

```python
def notification_required_capability(notification: str) -> str | None
```

The capability/sub-flag that gates ``notification``, or ``None`` if ungated.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.client_should_expect_notification"></a>

#### client\_should\_expect\_notification

```python
def client_should_expect_notification(notification: str,
                                      server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client should expect ``notification`` given the server's
capabilities. (R-6.3-h/-l/-o)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.compute_missing_client_capabilities"></a>

#### compute\_missing\_client\_capabilities

```python
def compute_missing_client_capabilities(declared: dict,
                                        required: dict) -> dict
```

Return the subset of ``required`` not present (by top-level key) in ``declared``.
Capabilities are never inferred from a prior request. (R-6.4-c/-d/-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.CapabilityGateResult"></a>

## CapabilityGateResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapabilityGateResult()
```

Outcome of :func:`gate_required_client_capabilities`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.gate_required_client_capabilities"></a>

#### gate\_required\_client\_capabilities

```python
def gate_required_client_capabilities(declared: dict,
                                      required: dict) -> CapabilityGateResult
```

Gate a request against the capabilities it requires (§6.4, R-6.4-h).

Returns ``ok=True`` when all required capabilities are declared, else ``ok=False`` with
the ``-32003`` error whose ``data.requiredCapabilities`` lists the missing ones.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.http_status_for_capability_error"></a>

#### http\_status\_for\_capability\_error

```python
def http_status_for_capability_error(code: int) -> int | None
```

Return ``400`` for the capability-negotiation error codes (``-32003`` / ``-32602``),
else ``None``. (R-6.4-i/-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_use_url_elicitation"></a>

#### may\_use\_url\_elicitation

```python
def may_use_url_elicitation(client_caps: dict) -> bool
```

A server MUST NOT use URL-mode elicitation unless ``elicitation.url`` is present. (R-6.2-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_use_sampling_tools"></a>

#### may\_use\_sampling\_tools

```python
def may_use_sampling_tools(client_caps: dict) -> bool
```

A server MUST NOT supply sampling tools unless ``sampling.tools`` is present. (R-6.2-q)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_invoke_roots_list"></a>

#### may\_invoke\_roots\_list

```python
def may_invoke_roots_list(client_caps: dict) -> bool
```

A server MUST NOT invoke ``roots/list`` unless ``roots`` is present. (R-6.2-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_invoke_sampling"></a>

#### may\_invoke\_sampling

```python
def may_invoke_sampling(client_caps: dict) -> bool
```

A server MUST NOT invoke ``sampling/createMessage`` unless ``sampling`` is present. (R-6.2-l)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.may_use_include_context"></a>

#### may\_use\_include\_context

```python
def may_use_include_context(client_caps: dict, value: str | None) -> bool
```

Whether a server MAY use a given ``includeContext`` value during sampling (R-6.2-o).

``None``/``"none"`` is always allowed; any other value needs ``sampling.context``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.capability_negotiation.decide_degradation"></a>

#### decide\_degradation

```python
def decide_degradation(*, peer_declares_behavior: bool,
                       behavior_mandatory: bool) -> str
```

Decide how to handle an operation when the peer may lack the optional behavior.

``proceed`` when the peer declares it; otherwise ``reject`` if mandatory else
``fallback``. A peer MUST NOT reject merely because the other declared fewer
capabilities. (R-6.4-l/-m)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.completion

Completion (§19).

Argument autocompletion: a best-effort, advisory facility a server offers so a client can
suggest ranked candidate values for a prompt argument or a resource-template variable. A
single ``completion/complete`` request carries the closed ``ref`` union + the partial
``argument`` (+ optional sibling ``context``); the server returns a ranked, ≤100-item list.
Gated by the ``completions`` capability.

The client-side keystroke debouncer (a UI convenience, §19.5 R-19.5-n) is provided by
:func:`create_completion_debouncer`, a thread-timer analogue of the JS ``setTimeout`` version.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.COMPLETION_METHOD_NOT_FOUND_CODE"></a>

#### COMPLETION\_METHOD\_NOT\_FOUND\_CODE

No `completions` capability → -32601. (§19.1, R-19.1-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.COMPLETION_INVALID_PARAMS_CODE"></a>

#### COMPLETION\_INVALID\_PARAMS\_CODE

Invalid params / unknown ref / unknown argument → -32602. (§19.5, R-19.5-r/-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.COMPLETION_INTERNAL_ERROR_CODE"></a>

#### COMPLETION\_INTERNAL\_ERROR\_CODE

Internal failure computing completions → -32603. (§19.5, R-19.5-t)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.MAX_COMPLETION_VALUES"></a>

#### MAX\_COMPLETION\_VALUES

Maximum items the completion.values array may carry. (§19.4, R-19.4-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.CompletionsCapability"></a>

```python
class CompletionsCapability(McpModel)
```

The ``completions`` capability value (§19.1) — an open object; ``{}`` is the RECOMMENDED
baseline. Extra members pass through.

Build the RECOMMENDED baseline ``completions`` capability — ``{}``. (§19.1, R-19.1-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.is_valid_complete_request_params"></a>

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.Completion"></a>

## Completion Objects

```python
class Completion(McpModel)
```

The ``completion`` object inside a ``CompleteResult`` (§19.4): a ``values`` list of ≤100
strings; OPTIONAL ``total`` (number) and ``hasMore`` (bool).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.CompleteResult"></a>

## CompleteResult Objects

```python
class CompleteResult(McpModel)
```

A ``completion/complete`` result (§19.4) — the Python analogue of the TS
``CompleteResultSchema``: a REQUIRED ``completion`` object + OPTIONAL ``_meta``.
``resultType`` is OPTIONAL and, when present, MUST be ``"complete"`` (absent ⇒ complete).


OPTIONAL ``total`` (number), ``hasMore`` (bool). (§19.4)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.is_valid_complete_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_complete\_result

```python
def is_valid_complete_result(value: object) -> bool
```

object; OPTIONAL ``_meta``. (§19.4)

#### resolve\_complete\_result\_type

```python
def resolve_complete_result_type(result: dict) -> str
```

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.completion.resolve_has_more"></a>

