```python
        input_responses: dict) -> InputResponseKeyValidation
```

Validate that every ``input_responses`` key was present in ``input_requests``.

Answering a subset is permitted (the client MAY answer fewer than asked); answering
an unknown key is a mismatch. (R-11.2-h, R-11.4-c, R-11.4-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.ElicitResult"></a>

## ElicitResult Objects

```python
class ElicitResult(McpModel)
```

``ElicitResult`` — client response to ``elicitation/create`` (§20). The S17-owned
constraint is the ``action`` discriminator; full shape is owned by S30/S31. (R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.ListRootsResult"></a>

## ListRootsResult Objects

```python
class ListRootsResult(McpModel)
```

``ListRootsResult`` — client response to ``roots/list`` (§21, deprecated). The S17-owned
constraint is the ``roots`` array; full shape is owned by S32. (R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.CreateMessageResult"></a>

## CreateMessageResult Objects

```python
class CreateMessageResult(McpModel)
```

``CreateMessageResult`` — client response to ``sampling/createMessage`` (§21, deprecated).
The S17-owned required fields are ``role``, ``content``, ``model``; full shape is owned by
S33. (R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.is_valid_elicit_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_elicit\_result

```python
def is_valid_elicit_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for an ``ElicitResult`` — response to ``elicitation/create``. (§20)

The S17-owned constraint is the ``action`` discriminator (``accept`` / ``decline`` /
``cancel``); OPTIONAL object ``content``. (R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.is_valid_list_roots_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_roots\_result

```python
def is_valid_list_roots_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a ``ListRootsResult`` — response to ``roots/list``. (§21, deprecated)

The S17-owned constraint is the ``roots`` array; each entry MUST carry a string ``uri``.
(R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.is_valid_create_message_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_create\_message\_result

```python
def is_valid_create_message_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a ``CreateMessageResult`` — response to ``sampling/createMessage``.
(§21, deprecated)

The S17-owned required fields are ``role`` (string), ``content`` (present), and ``model``
(string). (R-11.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.INPUT_RESPONSE_SCHEMA_BY_METHOD"></a>

#### INPUT\_RESPONSE\_SCHEMA\_BY\_METHOD

Maps an input-request ``method`` to the validator for the ``InputResponse`` kind the
client MUST answer with. Used by :func:`validate_input_response_kinds` to enforce
kind-correlation. (R-11.4-e, R-11.4-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.InputResponseKindError"></a>

## InputResponseKindError Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class InputResponseKindError()
```

One kind-correlation failure reported by :func:`validate_input_response_kinds`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.InputResponseKindValidation"></a>

## InputResponseKindValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class InputResponseKindValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_input_response_kinds`.

``valid`` is ``False`` when ``errors`` is non-empty.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.validate_input_response_kinds"></a>

#### validate\_input\_response\_kinds

```python
def validate_input_response_kinds(
        input_requests: dict,
        input_responses: dict) -> InputResponseKindValidation
```

Validate each ``input_responses`` value against the kind requested under its key.

Kind-correlation table (R-11.4-e):

* ``elicitation/create``     → ``ElicitResult``        (``action`` required)
* ``roots/list``             → ``ListRootsResult``     (``roots`` array required)
* ``sampling/createMessage`` → ``CreateMessageResult`` (``role``/``content``/``model``)

A client MUST NOT answer with a mismatched kind (R-11.4-f); servers reject such
responses with a JSON-RPC error (R-11.5-s). Precondition-hardened: a non-object
``input_responses`` carries no checkable correlations, so it is treated as valid here
(structural malformity is caught by the schema / key-presence checks) — never raises.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.build_malformed_retry_error"></a>

#### build\_malformed\_retry\_error

```python
def build_malformed_retry_error(detail: str) -> dict
```

Build the JSON-RPC error for a protocol-malformed retry request. (R-11.5-s)

A server MUST return a JSON-RPC error (NOT another ``input_required`` result) when
the retry's ``inputResponses`` is malformed at the protocol level. Code is ``-32602``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.RetryParamsValidation"></a>

## RetryParamsValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetryParamsValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_retry_params`.

``ok`` is ``True`` when every response passes kind-correlation; otherwise ``error``
carries the JSON-RPC error the server MUST return.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.validate_retry_params"></a>

#### validate\_retry\_params

```python
def validate_retry_params(input_requests: dict,
                          input_responses: dict) -> RetryParamsValidation
```

Validate server-side retry params, returning a JSON-RPC error when malformed. (R-11.5-s)

Returns ``ok=True`` when all response shapes pass kind-correlation; otherwise
``ok=False`` with a ``-32602`` error payload the server MUST return (never another
``input_required`` result).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.DUPLICATE_INPUT_REQUESTS_KEY_ERROR"></a>

#### DUPLICATE\_INPUT\_REQUESTS\_KEY\_ERROR

The JSON-RPC error for an ``input_required`` whose JSON repeats a member name. (R-11.2-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.ParsedInputRequiredResult"></a>

## ParsedInputRequiredResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ParsedInputRequiredResult()
```

Outcome of :func:`parse_input_required_result`.

``ok`` is ``True`` with ``result`` on success; otherwise ``error`` is the JSON-RPC
error payload.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.parse_input_required_result"></a>

#### parse\_input\_required\_result

```python
def parse_input_required_result(raw_json: str) -> ParsedInputRequiredResult
```

Parse an ``input_required`` result from raw JSON text, rejecting duplicate keys.

§11.2 requires a receiver encountering duplicate ``inputRequests`` keys to treat the
result as malformed (R-11.2-f) — stricter than the base §2.3.1 last-wins tolerance.
Duplicate detection runs on the raw text because ``json.loads`` would already have
collapsed repeats. Use this instead of ``json.loads`` + :func:`is_input_required_result`
when the raw wire text is available and duplicate-key strictness is required (TV-17.10).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.is_deprecated_input_request_kind"></a>

#### is\_deprecated\_input\_request\_kind

```python
def is_deprecated_input_request_kind(method: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``method`` is a Deprecated input-request kind. (§11.2, R-11.2-i)

Servers SHOULD prefer non-deprecated alternatives (e.g. ``elicitation/create``) over
soliciting via ``roots/list`` / ``sampling/createMessage``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.may_emit_input_request_kind"></a>

#### may\_emit\_input\_request\_kind

```python
def may_emit_input_request_kind(method: str,
                                client_capabilities: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server MAY emit an input-request of ``method``. (R-11.2-j, R-11.5-g)

A server MUST NOT emit a kind the client has not declared — withhold it and return
:func:`build_missing_capability_for_mrtr_error` instead. Equivalent to
:func:`client_supports_input_request_kind` (the same presence-means-supported check is
applied on both sides).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.compute_retry_backoff_ms"></a>

#### compute\_retry\_backoff\_ms

```python
def compute_retry_backoff_ms(attempt: int,
                             *,
                             base_ms: int = 250,
                             max_ms: int = 30_000) -> int
```

Compute an exponential-backoff delay (ms) for the Nth retry on non-progress. (R-11.5-n)

``attempt`` is the 1-based retry number (``attempt <= 0`` ⇒ ``0`` ms). The delay is
``base_ms * 2 ** (attempt - 1)`` clamped to ``max_ms``. A client retrying without
progress SHOULD apply a reasonable backoff (and offer the user a way to cancel).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.compute_missing_input_response_keys"></a>

#### compute\_missing\_input\_response\_keys

```python
def compute_missing_input_response_keys(input_requests: dict,
                                        input_responses: dict) -> list[str]
```

Return the ``input_requests`` keys the retry's ``input_responses`` left unanswered.
(§11.5, R-11.5-q)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.build_re_request_input_required_result"></a>

#### build\_re\_request\_input\_required\_result

```python
def build_re_request_input_required_result(
        input_requests: dict,
        input_responses: dict,
        request_state: str | None = None) -> dict | None
```

Build a NEW ``input_required`` re-requesting only the still-missing input, or ``None``.
(§11.5, R-11.5-q)

A server whose retry ``inputResponses`` is well-formed but incomplete SHOULD re-request
the missing information rather than failing the request. Returns ``None`` when the retry
supplied everything (the server completes instead).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.MrtrRoundGuard"></a>

## MrtrRoundGuard Objects

```python
class MrtrRoundGuard()
```

A bounded round counter guarding against an unbounded MRTR loop (no protocol
limit exists, so implementations SHOULD cap it). (§11.5, R-11.5-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.multi_round_trip.MrtrRoundGuard.record_round"></a>

#### record\_round

```python
def record_round() -> bool
```

Record one round; returns ``False`` once ``max_rounds`` is exceeded.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation

Revision selection & negotiation errors (§5.4–§5.7).

Turns discovery's raw materials (advertised revisions + capabilities) into a chosen
protocol revision and defines the recovery paths:

* the client revision-selection rule (§5.4): highest mutually supported revision, never
  fabricated; an empty intersection surfaces an actionable incompatibility.
* the two negotiation errors (§5.5/§5.6): ``UnsupportedProtocolVersion`` (-32004) and
  ``MissingRequiredClientCapability`` (-32003), both HTTP 400, plus client retries.
* the §5.7 backward-compatibility probe + a per-endpoint support-determination cache.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.NEGOTIATION_ERROR_HTTP_STATUS"></a>

#### NEGOTIATION\_ERROR\_HTTP\_STATUS

Both negotiation errors ride HTTP 400 Bad Request. (R-5.5-b, R-5.6-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.SERVER_DISCOVER_IS_OPTIONAL"></a>

#### SERVER\_DISCOVER\_IS\_OPTIONAL

server/discover is OPTIONAL before a first substantive request. (R-5.4-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.http_status_for_negotiation_error"></a>

#### http\_status\_for\_negotiation\_error

```python
def http_status_for_negotiation_error(code: int) -> int | None
```

Return ``400`` for the two negotiation error codes (-32004, -32003), else ``None``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.RevisionNegotiationResult"></a>

## RevisionNegotiationResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RevisionNegotiationResult()
```

Outcome of the revision-selection rule.

``ok`` with ``selected`` on success; otherwise ``reason="no-mutual-revision"`` with
both sides' revision lists.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.negotiate_revision"></a>

#### negotiate\_revision

```python
def negotiate_revision(
        client_preference: list[str],
        server_supported: list[str]) -> RevisionNegotiationResult
```

Select the highest mutually supported revision — the first in the client's order
that the server also supports (exact match). Empty intersection → failure; the client
MUST NOT fabricate a revision. (§5.4, R-5.4-b/-c/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.IncompatibleProtocolError"></a>

## IncompatibleProtocolError Objects

```python
class IncompatibleProtocolError(Exception)
```

An actionable error a client surfaces when no protocol revision is mutually
supported (never goes on the wire). (R-5.4-d, R-5.5-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.reselect_after_unsupported_version"></a>

#### reselect\_after\_unsupported\_version

```python
def reselect_after_unsupported_version(
        error: dict,
        client_preference: list[str]) -> RevisionNegotiationResult
```

React to a ``-32004`` error by re-selecting from its authoritative ``data.supported``
set. An empty result is terminal — the client MUST NOT retry indefinitely.
(R-5.5-h/-i/-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.can_satisfy_required_capabilities"></a>

#### can\_satisfy\_required\_capabilities

```python
def can_satisfy_required_capabilities(required_capabilities: dict,
                                      client_supported: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the client can declare every required capability key. (R-5.6-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.augment_client_capabilities"></a>

#### augment\_client\_capabilities

```python
def augment_client_capabilities(declared: dict,
                                required_capabilities: dict) -> dict
```

Produce the ``clientCapabilities`` for a retry after ``-32003``: the declared
capabilities merged with the required ones (shallow, non-mutating). (R-5.6-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.ProbeOutcome"></a>

## ProbeOutcome Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ProbeOutcome()
```

Outcome of interpreting a ``server/discover`` probe response (§5.7).

``kind`` is ``"supported"``, ``"unsupported-version"``, or ``"not-this-protocol"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.interpret_probe_response"></a>

#### interpret\_probe\_response

```python
def interpret_probe_response(response: object) -> ProbeOutcome
```

Interpret a probe ``server/discover`` response (§5.7).

A valid ``DiscoverResult`` → ``supported``; a recognized ``-32004`` carrying
``data.supported``/``data.requested`` → ``unsupported-version``; anything else
(different error, malformed, or ``None`` for a timeout) → ``not-this-protocol``.
(R-5.7-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.name_supported_revisions_in_error"></a>

#### name\_supported\_revisions\_in\_error

```python
def name_supported_revisions_in_error(base_error: dict,
                                      supported: list[str]) -> dict
```

Annotate an error's ``data.supported`` with the server's revisions so a peer with no
fall-forward mechanism still gets a useful diagnostic (non-mutating). (R-5.7-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.ProtocolSupportDetermination"></a>

## ProtocolSupportDetermination Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ProtocolSupportDetermination()
```

A per-endpoint conclusion about whether a server speaks this protocol family.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.ProtocolSupportCache"></a>

## ProtocolSupportCache Objects

```python
class ProtocolSupportCache()
```

Caches the protocol-support determination per server endpoint (R-5.7-e/-f).

The determination is a property of the endpoint, not a request; persist via
:meth:`entries` / :meth:`from_entries` and re-probe via :meth:`invalidate`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.negotiation.determination_from_probe"></a>

#### determination\_from\_probe

```python
def determination_from_probe(
        outcome: ProbeOutcome) -> ProtocolSupportDetermination
```

Derive a cacheable :class:`ProtocolSupportDetermination` from a probe outcome.
``supported`` and ``unsupported-version`` both mean the server speaks the family. (R-5.7-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination

Cursor-based pagination (§12).

Utilities for the uniform page-by-page exchange: cursor-presence / end-of-results
predicates, the per-cursor cache-key helper, the invalid-cursor (-32602) error, and a
reference offset paginator. The ``Cursor`` type is the opaque string from §3.7.

Paginated methods: ``tools/list``, ``resources/list``, ``resources/templates/list``,
``prompts/list``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.PaginatedRequestParams"></a>

## PaginatedRequestParams Objects

```python
class PaginatedRequestParams(McpModel)
```

The base parameter shape for any paginated list request (§12 / S18) — the Python
analogue of the TS ``PaginatedRequestParamsSchema``.

``cursor`` is OPTIONAL; when present it MUST be an opaque string (``""`` is a valid
PRESENT cursor, R-12.1-a). Method-specific members are tolerated.

Validates only the S18-owned base shape: the per-request ``_meta`` REQUIRED-key
constraint (§4.3 / S05) is enforced by ``is_valid_request_params`` when this rides a
client request, so a first-page request (``{}``) parses as valid here. (R-12.2-a/-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.PaginatedResult"></a>

## PaginatedResult Objects

```python
class PaginatedResult(McpModel)
```

The base result shape paginated list results extend (§12 / S18) — the Python analogue
of the TS ``PaginatedResultSchema``.

REQUIRED string ``resultType`` (the §3.6 discriminator); OPTIONAL opaque-string
``nextCursor`` (``""`` signals continuation, NOT end — R-12.3-d). Method-specific list
members are tolerated. (R-12.2-c, R-12.2-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.is_valid_paginated_request_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_paginated\_request\_params

```python
def is_valid_paginated_request_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid base paginated-request ``params``. (§12, §6 / S18)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.is_valid_paginated_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_paginated\_result

```python
def is_valid_paginated_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid base paginated ``result`` shape. (§12 / S18)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.has_next_cursor"></a>

#### has\_next\_cursor

```python
def has_next_cursor(result: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``nextCursor`` is present (even ``""``), i.e. more may follow.

The empty string is a PRESENT cursor — only absence signals the last page.
(R-12.2-c, R-12.3-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.is_last_page"></a>

#### is\_last\_page

```python
def is_last_page(result: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when this is the final page — ``nextCursor`` is absent. (R-12.2-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.is_cursor_present"></a>

#### is\_cursor\_present

```python
def is_cursor_present(cursor: str | None) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``cursor`` is a present value (including ``""``). Only ``None``
signals "no cursor". (R-12.1-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.build_invalid_cursor_error"></a>

#### build\_invalid\_cursor\_error

```python
def build_invalid_cursor_error(message: str | None = None) -> dict
```

Build the JSON-RPC error payload for an invalid-cursor rejection. (R-12.4-c/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.pagination_cache_key"></a>

#### pagination\_cache\_key

```python
def pagination_cache_key(method: str, cursor: str | None) -> str
```

Produce a per-page cache key. A page cached for one ``cursor`` MUST NOT be served
for a request bearing a different ``cursor`` (including the first-page request which
omits it). (R-12.5-a, R-13.5-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.PaginatorPage"></a>

## PaginatorPage Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PaginatorPage()
```

Outcome of :meth:`OffsetPaginator.get_page`.

On success ``ok`` is ``True`` with ``items`` + ``next_cursor``; on an unrecognized
cursor ``ok`` is ``False`` with a structured ``error`` (never raised).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.OffsetPaginator"></a>

## OffsetPaginator Objects

```python
class OffsetPaginator()
```

Reference cursor paginator over an in-memory list. Cursors are deterministic decimal
offset strings (stable); an unrecognized cursor yields a structured error, never raises.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.OffsetPaginator.get_page"></a>

#### get\_page

```python
def get_page(cursor: str | None) -> PaginatorPage
```

Return a page for ``cursor`` (absent → first page); unrecognized → error result.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.PAGINATED_METHODS"></a>

#### PAGINATED\_METHODS

Method names whose results carry paginated shapes. (§12)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.pagination.is_paginated_method"></a>

#### is\_paginated\_method

```python
def is_paginated_method(method: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``method`` is one of the paginated list methods.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.progress

Utilities: Progress & Cancellation (§15.1–§15.2).

Two cross-cutting utility mechanisms layered on any request:

* Out-of-band progress reporting via ``notifications/progress`` (§15.1).
* Request cancellation via ``notifications/cancelled`` (§15.2).

Both are optional, opt-in, fire-and-forget mechanisms. A peer that does not implement
either continues to operate correctly. (R-15-a)

Progress is request-scoped: notifications travel on the response stream of the request
whose ``_meta.progressToken`` opted in, before the final response. Cancellation is
same-direction-only: a party may cancel only requests it issued, never requests it
received.

The TypeScript SDK expresses the wire shapes as Zod schemas; this port translates each
schema into a ``is_valid_*`` predicate plus a matching ``build_*`` constructor (dicts in,
``bool`` / ``dict`` out), and ports the stateful helper classes faithfully.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_valid_progress_token"></a>

#### is\_valid\_progress\_token

Re-export so callers can validate a ``ProgressToken`` from this module directly. A
progress token is an opaque string or number; ``bool`` is rejected. (R-15.1.1-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.StringOrNumber"></a>

#### StringOrNumber

An opaque ``string | number`` progress token / request id — booleans rejected, like the
TS ``ProgressTokenSchema`` / ``RequestIdSchema``. (R-15.1.1-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.PROGRESS_NOTIFICATION_METHOD"></a>

#### PROGRESS\_NOTIFICATION\_METHOD

Method name for the progress notification. (§15.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CANCELLED_NOTIFICATION_METHOD"></a>

#### CANCELLED\_NOTIFICATION\_METHOD

Method name for the cancellation notification. (§15.2)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.JSONRPC_VERSION"></a>

#### JSONRPC\_VERSION

The JSON-RPC version marker every notification envelope MUST carry. (§3)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressNotificationParams"></a>

## ProgressNotificationParams Objects

```python
class ProgressNotificationParams(McpModel)
```

``notifications/progress`` params (§15.1.3) — the Python analogue of the TS
``ProgressNotificationParamsSchema``.

``progressToken`` (REQUIRED) correlates to the opted-in request; ``progress`` (REQUIRED)
is a number that MUST strictly increase across successive notifications (monotonicity is
enforced at runtime by :class:`ProgressTracker`); ``total`` / ``message`` are OPTIONAL.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_valid_progress_notification_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_progress\_notification\_params

```python
def is_valid_progress_notification_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``notifications/progress`` params. (§15.1.3)

``progressToken`` + ``progress`` REQUIRED; ``total`` (number) / ``message`` (string)
OPTIONAL. (R-15.1.3-a/-d/-g/-j) See :class:`ProgressNotificationParams`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.build_progress_notification_params"></a>

#### build\_progress\_notification\_params

```python
def build_progress_notification_params(progress_token: str | int | float,
                                       progress: int | float,
                                       *,
                                       total: int | float | None = None,
                                       message: str | None = None,
                                       meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build ``notifications/progress`` params; optional fields only when supplied. (§15.1.3)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``progress_token`` is not a valid progress token, or when
``progress`` / ``total`` are not numbers.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_valid_progress_notification"></a>

#### is\_valid\_progress\_notification

```python
def is_valid_progress_notification(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a full ``notifications/progress`` notification envelope. (§15.1)

``jsonrpc`` is exactly ``"2.0"``, ``method`` is :data:`PROGRESS_NOTIFICATION_METHOD`,
and ``params`` is a valid progress-notification params object.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.build_progress_notification"></a>

#### build\_progress\_notification

```python
def build_progress_notification(progress_token: str | int | float,
                                progress: int | float,
                                *,
                                total: int | float | None = None,
                                message: str | None = None,
                                meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a complete ``notifications/progress`` notification envelope. (§15.1)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when the params are invalid (see
:func:`build_progress_notification_params`).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledNotificationParams"></a>

## CancelledNotificationParams Objects

```python
class CancelledNotificationParams(McpModel)
```

``notifications/cancelled`` params (§15.2.1) — the Python analogue of the TS
``CancelledNotificationParamsSchema``.

``requestId`` is OPTIONAL in the schema *shape* so a receiver tolerates malformed
cancellations gracefully (R-15.2.2-f); when present it MUST be a string or number
referencing an in-flight request the sender issued (R-15.2.1-a/-b). ``reason`` (OPTIONAL)
is a human-readable string (R-15.2.1-c).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_valid_cancelled_notification_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_cancelled\_notification\_params

```python
def is_valid_cancelled_notification_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``notifications/cancelled`` params. (§15.2.1)

``requestId`` OPTIONAL (string or number when present); ``reason`` OPTIONAL string. See
:class:`CancelledNotificationParams`. (R-15.2.1-a/-b/-c, R-15.2.2-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.build_cancelled_notification_params"></a>

#### build\_cancelled\_notification\_params

```python
def build_cancelled_notification_params(request_id: str | int | float
                                        | None = None,
                                        *,
                                        reason: str | None = None,
                                        meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build ``notifications/cancelled`` params; optional fields only when supplied. (§15.2.1)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``request_id`` is supplied but is not a string or number, or
when ``reason`` is supplied but is not a string.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_valid_cancelled_notification"></a>

#### is\_valid\_cancelled\_notification

```python
def is_valid_cancelled_notification(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a full ``notifications/cancelled`` notification envelope. (§15.2)

``jsonrpc`` is exactly ``"2.0"``, ``method`` is :data:`CANCELLED_NOTIFICATION_METHOD`,
and ``params`` is a valid cancelled-notification params object.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.build_cancelled_notification"></a>

#### build\_cancelled\_notification

```python
def build_cancelled_notification(request_id: str | int | float | None = None,
                                 *,
                                 reason: str | None = None,
                                 meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a complete ``notifications/cancelled`` notification envelope. (§15.2)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when the params are invalid (see
:func:`build_cancelled_notification_params`).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker"></a>

## ProgressTracker Objects

```python
class ProgressTracker()
```

Tracks active progress tokens for a single sender, enforcing the uniqueness and
monotonicity rules of §15.1.

Rules enforced:

* R-15.1.1-c — tokens MUST be unique across the sender's currently active requests.
* R-15.1.1-d — receivers MUST treat the token as opaque (no content inspection).
* R-15.1.3-e — ``progress`` MUST strictly increase across successive notifications.
* R-15.1.4-g — MUST stop emitting progress once the operation reaches a terminal state.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.register"></a>

#### register

```python
def register(token: str | int | float) -> None
```

Register ``token`` as active when a request carrying it is about to be sent.

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``token`` is already active — enforces R-15.1.1-c.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.complete"></a>

#### complete

```python
def complete(token: str | int | float) -> None
```

Remove ``token`` from the active set once the operation reached a terminal state
(final response sent or received). (R-15.1.4-g)

Safe to call for a token that is not currently tracked.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.has"></a>

#### has

```python
def has(token: str | int | float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``token`` is currently registered as active.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.is_monotonic"></a>

#### is\_monotonic

```python
def is_monotonic(token: str | int | float, progress: int | float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``progress`` is strictly greater than the last recorded value
for ``token``, satisfying the monotonic-increase invariant. (R-15.1.3-e)

Returns ``False`` for an unknown (not-yet-registered or already-completed) token.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.record_progress"></a>

#### record\_progress

```python
def record_progress(token: str | int | float, progress: int | float) -> None
```

Record ``progress`` as the latest value for ``token`` after a monotonicity check

has passed.

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``token`` is not currently active.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.size"></a>

#### size

```python
@property
def size() -> int
```

Number of currently active progress tokens.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressTracker.active_tokens"></a>

#### active\_tokens

```python
@property
def active_tokens() -> list
```

Snapshot of all currently active tokens (original string/number values).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressRateLimiter"></a>

## ProgressRateLimiter Objects

```python
class ProgressRateLimiter()
```

Per-token rate-limiter for ``notifications/progress`` emissions. (RC-3 / SHOULD)

Implementations SHOULD throttle progress notifications to avoid flooding the transport.
A sender may call :meth:`should_emit` before dispatching each notification; the limiter
suppresses emissions that arrive within the quiet window for that token. Each token has
an independent time-of-last-emission so a slow-moving token is not penalized by a
fast-moving one.

Example::

limiter = ProgressRateLimiter(100)  # 100 ms minimum interval
if limiter.should_emit(token, now_ms):
send_progress_notification(token, progress)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressRateLimiter.__init__"></a>

#### \_\_init\_\_

```python
def __init__(interval_ms: int | float = 100) -> None
```

**Arguments**:

- `interval_ms`: minimum milliseconds between successive progress notifications
for the same token. Defaults to 100 ms. (RC-3)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressRateLimiter.should_emit"></a>

#### should\_emit

```python
def should_emit(token: str | int | float, now_ms: int | float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a notification for ``token`` may be emitted at ``now_ms``.

Calling this records ``now_ms`` as the last-emit time for the token when emission is
permitted, so the next call is automatically constrained.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.ProgressRateLimiter.complete"></a>

#### complete

```python
def complete(token: str | int | float) -> None
```

Clear the rate-limit state for ``token`` when the operation is terminal.

Safe to call for an unknown token.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler"></a>

## CancellationHandler Objects

```python
class CancellationHandler()
```

Receiver-side registry mapping in-flight request ids to abort callbacks.
(R-15.2.2-d / RC-4)

When a valid ``notifications/cancelled`` arrives, the receiver SHOULD stop processing
the matching request, free associated resources, and suppress sending the response.
This wires that behaviour:

1. **Register** — before dispatching a long-running request, register an abort callback.
2. **Trigger** — when a valid cancellation arrives (after
   :func:`validate_cancellation_target` confirms eligibility), call :meth:`trigger` to
   fire the callback and deregister the entry.
3. **Deregister** — on normal completion, call :meth:`deregister` to remove the entry
   without firing the callback.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler.register"></a>

#### register

```python
def register(request_id: str | int | float, on_cancel) -> None
```

Register ``on_cancel`` as the abort callback for ``request_id``.

A previously registered handler for the same id is silently replaced — callers should
:meth:`deregister` before re-using an id.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler.trigger"></a>

#### trigger

```python
def trigger(request_id: str | int | float) -> bool
```

Fire the abort callback for ``request_id`` and remove it from the registry.

Returns ``True`` when a handler was found and called (the request was stopped);
``False`` when no handler is registered — the cancellation may have arrived after the
work already completed.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler.deregister"></a>

#### deregister

```python
def deregister(request_id: str | int | float) -> None
```

Remove the handler for ``request_id`` without calling it.

Call this on normal completion so the registry does not hold stale entries. Safe to
call for an unknown ``request_id``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler.has"></a>

#### has

```python
def has(request_id: str | int | float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when an abort callback is registered for ``request_id``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationHandler.size"></a>

#### size

```python
@property
def size() -> int
```

Number of currently registered abort callbacks.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledRequestSet"></a>

## CancelledRequestSet Objects

```python
class CancelledRequestSet()
```

Sender-side set of request ids for which a ``notifications/cancelled`` has been sent
but whose response has not yet arrived. (R-15.2.3-e / RC-6)

A sender SHOULD distinctly ignore (not merely tolerate) late responses to cancelled
requests, so callers can detect the race rather than silently process a stale result.

Usage:

1. :meth:`add` — call immediately after sending the cancellation notification.
2. :meth:`is_ignorable` — call when a response arrives; if ``True``, discard it.
3. :meth:`acknowledge` — call after discarding the late response to bound set growth.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledRequestSet.add"></a>

#### add

```python
def add(request_id: str | int | float) -> None
```

Mark ``request_id`` as cancelled.

Call this after sending ``notifications/cancelled`` for the request.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledRequestSet.is_ignorable"></a>

#### is\_ignorable

```python
def is_ignorable(request_id: str | int | float) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a response for ``request_id`` SHOULD be ignored because a
cancellation notification was previously sent for it. (R-15.2.3-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledRequestSet.acknowledge"></a>

#### acknowledge

```python
def acknowledge(request_id: str | int | float) -> None
```

Remove ``request_id`` from the set after the late response was received and
discarded. Safe to call for an unknown ``request_id``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancelledRequestSet.size"></a>

#### size

```python
@property
def size() -> int
```

Number of ids awaiting a late response to discard.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.SERVER_DISCOVER_METHOD"></a>

#### SERVER\_DISCOVER\_METHOD

The method name for the ``server/discover`` handshake exchange. Clients MUST NOT
cancel this exchange. (R-15.2.2-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.is_discover_method"></a>

#### is\_discover\_method

```python
def is_discover_method(method: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``method`` names the ``server/discover`` handshake, which MUST
NOT be cancelled by a client. (R-15.2.2-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.CancellationValidationResult"></a>

## CancellationValidationResult Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CancellationValidationResult()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_cancellation_target`.

``ok=True`` means the target is eligible for cancellation; otherwise ``reason`` explains
why it is not.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.progress.validate_cancellation_target"></a>

#### validate\_cancellation\_target

```python
def validate_cancellation_target(
    request_id: str | int | float | None,
    in_flight_ids,
    discover_request_id: str | int | float | None = None
) -> CancellationValidationResult
```

Validate that a cancellation target (``requestId`` from a ``notifications/cancelled``)

is eligible given the sender's in-flight set. (R-15.2.1-a, R-15.2.1-b, R-15.2.2-b)

A valid target must:

* be present (``request_id`` is not ``None``),
* appear in ``in_flight_ids`` (in-flight from the sender's perspective),
* not be the ``server/discover`` id (when ``discover_request_id`` is provided).

**Arguments**:

- `request_id`: the target id from the cancellation notification.
- `in_flight_ids`: a set/container of ids the sender has issued and not yet received
a response to.
- `discover_request_id`: if provided, the id of the ``server/discover`` request that
must not be cancelled.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts

Prompts (§18).

Server-offered templates that produce structured conversation messages. A server exposes
named prompts (optionally accepting arguments) discovered via ``prompts/list`` and
retrieved via ``prompts/get``. This module fixes the capability + gating, the ``Prompt`` /
``PromptArgument`` / ``PromptMessage`` shapes, the paginated/cacheable list result, the
argument-rendered get result + its ``input_required`` discrimination, the ``-32602`` /
``-32603`` error model, and the list-changed notification.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.MRTR_RESULT_TYPE_INPUT_REQUIRED"></a>

#### MRTR\_RESULT\_TYPE\_INPUT\_REQUIRED

Re-export the canonical multi-round-trip "input_required" discriminator value and
input-required validator (the SAME S17 bindings) so prompt callers handling a
``prompts/get`` retry need not reach into multi_round_trip directly. Mirrors the TS
``MRTR_RESULT_TYPE`` / ``InputRequiredResultSchema`` re-exports from prompts.ts.
The multi-round-trip ``input_required`` discriminator value. (§11, S17)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompts_input_required_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompts\_input\_required\_result

Validator for a well-formed ``InputRequiredResult`` — the S17 binding a ``prompts/get``
retry handler checks an ``input_required`` body against. (§11, S17)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.PROMPTS_INVALID_PARAMS_CODE"></a>

#### PROMPTS\_INVALID\_PARAMS\_CODE

Unknown prompt name / missing required argument → -32602. (§18.4, R-18.4-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.PROMPTS_INTERNAL_ERROR_CODE"></a>

#### PROMPTS\_INTERNAL\_ERROR\_CODE

An internal failure resolving prompts/get → -32603. (§18.4, R-18.4-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.PromptsCapability"></a>

## PromptsCapability Objects

```python
class PromptsCapability(McpModel)
```

The ``prompts`` capability value (§18.1) — OPTIONAL strict-boolean ``listChanged``;
empty ``{}`` valid; extra members pass through.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompts_capability"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompts\_capability

```python
def is_valid_prompts_capability(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``prompts`` capability: OPTIONAL boolean ``listChanged``;
empty ``{}`` valid. (§18.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.server_declares_prompts"></a>

#### server\_declares\_prompts

```python
def server_declares_prompts(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server declares the ``prompts`` capability. (§18.1, R-18.1-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.may_call_prompt_method"></a>

#### may\_call\_prompt\_method

```python
def may_call_prompt_method(method: str, server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY send ``method`` (``prompts/list``/``prompts/get``)
given the server's capabilities. (§18.1, R-18.1-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.may_expect_prompts_list_changed"></a>

#### may\_expect\_prompts\_list\_changed

```python
def may_expect_prompts_list_changed(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client may expect ``notifications/prompts/list_changed`` (needs
``prompts.listChanged``). (§18.1, R-18.1-e/-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.PromptArgument"></a>

## PromptArgument Objects

```python
class PromptArgument(BaseMetadata)
```

An argument a prompt accepts (§18.3) — ``BaseMetadata`` + OPTIONAL ``description`` and
strict-boolean ``required``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.Prompt"></a>

## Prompt Objects

```python
class Prompt(BaseMetadata)
```

A prompt template descriptor (§18.3) — the Python analogue of the TS ``PromptSchema``.

``BaseMetadata`` + OPTIONAL ``description``, ``arguments`` (``PromptArgument`` list),
``icons``, and ``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompt_argument"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompt\_argument

```python
def is_valid_prompt_argument(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``PromptArgument`` (§18.3): ``BaseMetadata`` + OPTIONAL
``description`` (str) and ``required`` (bool).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompt"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompt

```python
def is_valid_prompt(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``Prompt`` (§18.3): ``BaseMetadata`` + OPTIONAL
``description`` (str), ``arguments`` (list of PromptArgument), ``icons`` (list), ``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.required_argument_names"></a>

#### required\_argument\_names

```python
def required_argument_names(prompt: dict) -> list[str]
```

Return the names of arguments declared ``required: true``. (R-18.3-l, R-18.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.PromptMessage"></a>

## PromptMessage Objects

```python
class PromptMessage(McpModel)
```

One conversation message in a prompt (§18.5) — a ``role`` plus exactly one ``content``
block (a single object, not an array).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompt_message"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompt\_message

```python
def is_valid_prompt_message(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``PromptMessage`` (§18.5): a ``role`` + exactly one
``content`` block (a single object, not an array).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_list_prompts_request_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_prompts\_request\_params

```python
def is_valid_list_prompts_request_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``prompts/list`` request ``params`` (§18.2).

This is the paginated request shape (S18): an OPTIONAL opaque ``cursor`` (``""`` is a
valid PRESENT cursor; a client MUST NOT construct/parse/modify it — it is echoed back
verbatim from a prior ``nextCursor``) and an OPTIONAL ``_meta`` object. The cursor MAY
be omitted entirely (a first-page request is ``{}``). Reuses
:func:`is_valid_paginated_request_params` rather than redefining the cursor field.
(R-18.2-a, R-18.2-b, R-18.2-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.resolve_list_prompts_result_type"></a>

#### resolve\_list\_prompts\_result\_type

```python
def resolve_list_prompts_result_type(result: dict) -> str
```

Resolve a ``prompts/list`` result's ``resultType``, treating absent as ``"complete"``.
(R-18.2-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.ListPromptsResult"></a>

## ListPromptsResult Objects

```python
class ListPromptsResult(McpModel)
```

The result of ``prompts/list`` (§18.2) — a paginated + cacheable result wrapping the
REQUIRED ``prompts`` page. The Python analogue of the TS ``ListPromptsResultSchema``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_list_prompts_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_prompts\_result

```python
def is_valid_list_prompts_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``ListPromptsResult`` (§18.2): ``resultType``
``"complete"``, ``prompts`` list of valid Prompts, OPTIONAL ``nextCursor``, REQUIRED
non-negative ``ttlMs`` + ``cacheScope``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.ListPromptsResultConfig"></a>

## ListPromptsResultConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ListPromptsResultConfig()
```

Server-supplied inputs to a ``ListPromptsResult``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_list_prompts_result"></a>

#### build\_list\_prompts\_result

```python
def build_list_prompts_result(config: ListPromptsResultConfig) -> dict
```

Build a completed ``ListPromptsResult``; optional fields only when supplied. (§18.2)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``ttl_ms`` is negative or not an integer. (R-18.2-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_get_prompt_request_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_get\_prompt\_request\_params

```python
def is_valid_get_prompt_request_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``prompts/get`` request ``params`` (§18.4).

Field constraints (R-18.4-a – R-18.4-k):

* ``name`` REQUIRED string — the prompt to retrieve; matches a ``Prompt.name``.
* ``arguments`` OPTIONAL map<string,string> — values keyed by ``PromptArgument.name``;
  each value MUST be a string.
* ``inputResponses`` OPTIONAL object — multi-round-trip retry responses (§11); omitted
  on a first attempt.
* ``requestState`` OPTIONAL opaque string — echoed verbatim on retry; omitted on a
  first attempt.
* ``_meta`` REQUIRED object — every client request carries per-request metadata (S04),
  so it is modeled as required here (matching ``is_valid_request_params``).

Additional members are tolerated (the model uses ``extra="allow"``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptRequestParams"></a>

## GetPromptRequestParams Objects

```python
class GetPromptRequestParams(McpModel)
```

The ``params`` of a ``prompts/get`` request (§18.4) — the Python analogue of the TS
``GetPromptRequestParamsSchema``.

``name`` REQUIRED; ``arguments`` OPTIONAL ``map<string,string>``; OPTIONAL multi-round-trip
retry fields (``inputResponses`` / ``requestState``); ``_meta`` REQUIRED per-request
metadata. (R-18.4-a – R-18.4-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.resolve_get_prompt_result_type"></a>

#### resolve\_get\_prompt\_result\_type

```python
def resolve_get_prompt_result_type(result: dict) -> str
```

Resolve a ``prompts/get`` result's ``resultType``, treating absent as ``"complete"``.
(R-18.4-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptResult"></a>

## GetPromptResult Objects

```python
class GetPromptResult(McpModel)
```

A completed ``prompts/get`` result (§18.4) — the Python analogue of the TS
``GetPromptResultSchema``.

``messages`` (REQUIRED ``PromptMessage`` list); OPTIONAL ``description`` and ``_meta``.
``resultType`` is OPTIONAL and, when present, MUST be ``"complete"`` (an absent value is
treated as ``"complete"`` per §3.6; the ``"input_required"`` variant is the S17 result).

Intentional divergence from the TS ``GetPromptResultSchema`` (an accepted, documented
decision rather than an oversight): TS types ``resultType`` as the broad, REQUIRED
``ResultTypeSchema`` (``z.string()``). Here it is pinned to the single value a *completed*
prompt result may carry, ``"complete"``, and made optional so an absent discriminator is
tolerated per R-18.4-p — a stricter, more precise contract than the open string while
remaining behaviourally equivalent on every test vector. The non-``complete``
``"input_required"`` case is the separate S17 ``InputRequiredResult`` and is discriminated
upstream, never validated as a ``GetPromptResult``. (R-18.4-n, R-18.4-o, R-18.4-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_get_prompt_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_get\_prompt\_result

```python
def is_valid_get_prompt_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed (completed) ``GetPromptResult`` (§18.4): a
``messages`` list of valid PromptMessages; OPTIONAL ``description`` (str), ``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptResultConfig"></a>

## GetPromptResultConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GetPromptResultConfig()
```

Server-supplied inputs to a completed ``GetPromptResult``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_get_prompt_result"></a>

#### build\_get\_prompt\_result

```python
def build_get_prompt_result(config: GetPromptResultConfig) -> dict
```

Build a completed ``GetPromptResult`` (``resultType: "complete"``); optional fields
only when supplied. (§18.4)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptResponseDiscrimination"></a>

## GetPromptResponseDiscrimination Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GetPromptResponseDiscrimination()
```

What a client should do after a ``prompts/get`` response (branch on ``resultType``
before parsing the body, R-18.4-r).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptResponseDiscrimination.kind"></a>

#### kind

"complete" | "input_required" | "error"

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.discriminate_get_prompt_response"></a>

#### discriminate\_get\_prompt\_response

```python
def discriminate_get_prompt_response(
        response: object) -> GetPromptResponseDiscrimination
```

Branch a ``prompts/get`` response on its ``resultType`` (§18.4/§11): ``"complete"``
(or absent) + a well-formed body → complete; ``"input_required"`` + a well-formed
``InputRequiredResult`` → input_required; any unrecognized ``resultType`` or a body that
fails its schema → error. (R-18.4-q/-r)

Reuses :func:`discriminate_result_type` (S17) for the result-type branching so the
§3.6/§11.5 receiver rules (absent ⇒ complete; unrecognized ⇒ error; malformed
``input_required`` ⇒ error) apply uniformly, then validates the completed body against
:func:`is_valid_get_prompt_result`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_unknown_prompt_error"></a>

#### build\_unknown\_prompt\_error

```python
def build_unknown_prompt_error(name: str) -> dict
```

Build the -32602 error for an unknown prompt name. (§18.4, R-18.4-d/-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_missing_argument_error"></a>

#### build\_missing\_argument\_error

```python
def build_missing_argument_error(missing: list[str]) -> dict
```

Build the -32602 error for omitted required arguments. (§18.3/§18.4, R-18.4-g/-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_prompt_internal_error"></a>

#### build\_prompt\_internal\_error

```python
def build_prompt_internal_error(detail: str | None = None) -> dict
```

Build the -32603 internal error for a prompts/get resolution failure. (§18.4, R-18.4-s)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.GetPromptRequestValidation"></a>

## GetPromptRequestValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GetPromptRequestValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_get_prompt_request`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.validate_get_prompt_request"></a>

#### validate\_get\_prompt\_request

```python
def validate_get_prompt_request(params: dict,
                                offered) -> GetPromptRequestValidation
```

Validate a ``prompts/get`` request: it MUST name an offered prompt and supply every
``required: true`` argument (unknown-name check first). On failure returns the mapped
-32602 error. (§18.4, R-18.4-c–R-18.4-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompt_list_changed_notification_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompt\_list\_changed\_notification\_params

```python
def is_valid_prompt_list_changed_notification_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``notifications/prompts/list_changed`` ``params`` (§18.6).

When present, the params object MAY carry ONLY a reserved ``_meta`` map (an object) and
no prompt data; the notification itself carries no prompt payload. Additional
forward-compatible members are tolerated (the TS schema uses ``.passthrough()``).
(R-18.6-c, AC-28.40)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.is_valid_prompt_list_changed_notification"></a>

#### is\_valid\_prompt\_list\_changed\_notification

```python
def is_valid_prompt_list_changed_notification(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``notifications/prompts/list_changed`` (no ``id``;
optional ``_meta``-only params). (§18.6)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.build_prompt_list_changed_notification"></a>

#### build\_prompt\_list\_changed\_notification

```python
def build_prompt_list_changed_notification(meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a ``notifications/prompts/list_changed``; ``params`` only when ``meta`` supplied.
A server SHOULD emit it only when it declared ``prompts.listChanged: true``. (§18.6)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.prompts.may_complete_prompt_argument"></a>

#### may\_complete\_prompt\_argument

```python
def may_complete_prompt_argument() -> bool
```

Whether a client MAY request completion for a prompt argument value — always ``True``;
prompt argument values are always completable. (§18.7, R-18.7-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.registries

Consolidated Registries: Methods, Errors, ``_meta`` Keys, Capabilities, and Types
(Appendices A–E).

The capstone reference artifact: five authoritative, document-wide tables that
enumerate the wire surface defined across the whole specification, each row pointing to
the section that normatively specifies the entry. These appendices define no new wire
types — they are a *consolidation*, not a new definition, and the cited section remains
normative.

This module REUSES existing bindings rather than redefining them:

* The §22 / Appendix B error-code registry is re-exported from :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.errors`
  (``ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY``, ``RESERVED_ERROR_CODES``, ``validate_extension_error_code``,
  ``SERVER_ERROR_RANGE``); it is never rebuilt here. (Appendix B; R-AppB-a, R-AppB-b)
* The reserved-bare-key set comes from :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.meta` (``RESERVED_BARE_KEYS``)
  and the prefix-reservation predicate / parser come from :mod:`stackific.mcp.json.meta_key`
  (``is_reserved_meta_key_prefix``, ``parse_meta_key``).

The four NEW registries (methods, ``_meta`` keys, capabilities, types) are expressed as
DATA structures over the literal wire names plus their metadata (direction, kind,
capability gating, owning section), mirroring the registry-as-data style of
:mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.errors`. Method/key/capability names appear as string literals in the
data — this is deliberate and safer than importing the scattered method-name constants
from a dozen sibling modules.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CustomErrorCodeValidation"></a>

## CustomErrorCodeValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CustomErrorCodeValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_custom_error_code`.

``ok=True`` means the code is usable; ``in_reserved_range`` then says whether the code
lies inside the reserved server-error range ``-32000..-32099``. ``ok=False`` carries a
machine-readable ``reason``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.validate_custom_error_code"></a>

#### validate\_custom\_error\_code

```python
def validate_custom_error_code(code: object) -> CustomErrorCodeValidation
```

Validate a custom error ``code`` against Appendix B's collision rule.

A custom code MUST NOT equal any code listed in the Error Code Registry (the five
standard JSON-RPC codes, the two protocol codes, and ``-32001`` HeaderMismatch).
(R-AppB-a, AC-46.1)

Codes inside the reserved server-error range ``-32000..-32099`` are permitted only
when they avoid collision with a code this document defines (notably ``-32001``);
``-32000..-32099`` is the range in which additions are explicitly allowed. (R-AppB-b,
AC-46.2)

Returns ``ok=True`` when the code is usable (with ``in_reserved_range`` set), otherwise
``ok=False`` with a ``reason``. Delegates the integer/collision check to
:func:`mcp.protocol.errors.validate_extension_error_code` (the §22 helper) so the two
stay in lockstep.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.RegistryMethodKind"></a>

## RegistryMethodKind Objects

```python
class RegistryMethodKind(StrEnum)
```

The **Kind** column of Appendix A: whether a name is a request (expects a
response), a notification (no response), or an input-request kind delivered embedded
in an input-required result (§11) rather than as a standalone server-initiated
request. (Appendix A)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.RegistryMethodKind.REQUEST"></a>

#### REQUEST

A request that expects a response.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.RegistryMethodKind.NOTIFICATION"></a>

#### NOTIFICATION

A notification — no response is sent.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.RegistryMethodKind.INPUT_REQUEST"></a>

#### INPUT\_REQUEST

An input-request kind (``elicitation/create``, ``sampling/createMessage``,
``roots/list``): delivered inside an input-required result and resolved by client
retry (§11); NOT a standalone server-initiated JSON-RPC request.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry"></a>

## MethodNotificationIndexEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MethodNotificationIndexEntry()
```

One row of Appendix A — a single method or notification name.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry.name"></a>

#### name

The JSON-RPC method or notification name (for example ``tools/list``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry.kind"></a>

#### kind

Whether the name is a request, a notification, or an input-request kind.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry.direction"></a>

#### direction

The normal sender→receiver pairing (for example ``client→server``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry.defined_in"></a>

#### defined\_in

The section that normatively defines the message.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MethodNotificationIndexEntry.extension_scoped"></a>

#### extension\_scoped

When ``True``, the name is only in scope while the named extension is active.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.METHOD_REGISTRY"></a>

#### METHOD\_REGISTRY

Appendix A — the Method and Notification Index: every JSON-RPC method and notification
defined by the document and its extensions, with its kind, direction, and defining
section. (Appendix A)

The three input-request kinds (``elicitation/create``, ``sampling/createMessage``,
``roots/list``) are delivered embedded in an input-required result and are NOT
standalone server-initiated requests (see :class:`RegistryMethodKind`). The two
trailing ``UI↔host`` handshake rows (§26) are in scope only when the UI extension is
active; they carry ``extension_scoped=True``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.UI_DIALECT_METHOD_INDEX"></a>

#### UI\_DIALECT\_METHOD\_INDEX

The additional UI-dialect message names (§26) exchanged on the UI message channel
(``UI↔host``), in scope ONLY when the user-interface extension is active — beyond the
two handshake names already in :data:`METHOD_REGISTRY`. Named ``..._INDEX`` to stay
distinct from the method-name constant map owned by the UI module. Recorded
separately because they are conditional on the extension. (Appendix A)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.lookup_method"></a>

#### lookup\_method

```python
def lookup_method(
        name: str,
        include_ui_dialect: bool = False
) -> MethodNotificationIndexEntry | None
```

Look up the Appendix A entry for a method or notification ``name``.

Searches the core index first and (when ``include_ui_dialect`` is ``True``) the
UI-dialect names. Returns ``None`` when the name is not in the index. (Appendix A)

Because a handful of UI-dialect names (``tools/call``, ``resources/read``,
``notifications/message``) shadow core names, the core index is preferred unless a core
hit is absent. To inspect a UI-dialect-only meaning, pass ``include_ui_dialect=True``
and read the returned ``direction``/``defined_in``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.is_registered_method"></a>

#### is\_registered\_method

```python
def is_registered_method(name: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``name`` appears in the core Appendix A index.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry"></a>

## MetaKeyRegistryEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MetaKeyRegistryEntry()
```

One row of Appendix C — a reserved key that MAY appear in ``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.key"></a>

#### key

The reserved ``_meta`` key (prefixed or bare-by-exception).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.used_on"></a>

#### used\_on

Where the key normally appears.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.meaning"></a>

#### meaning

Purpose, requirement level, and deprecation status where applicable.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.defined_in"></a>

#### defined\_in

The section that normatively specifies the key.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.requirement"></a>

#### requirement

Requirement level on the location named in ``used_on``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.MetaKeyRegistryEntry.deprecated"></a>

#### deprecated

When ``True``, the key carries Deprecated status.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.META_KEY_REGISTRY"></a>

#### META\_KEY\_REGISTRY

Appendix C — the Reserved ``_meta`` Key Registry: every key reserved by this document
that MAY appear in ``_meta`` (the ``io.modelcontextprotocol/`` prefixed keys plus the
four bare-by-exception keys), each with where it is used, its meaning/requirement
level, and its defining section. (Appendix C; R-AppC-a … j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.lookup_meta_key"></a>

#### lookup\_meta\_key

```python
def lookup_meta_key(key: str) -> MetaKeyRegistryEntry | None
```

Look up the Appendix C entry for an exact reserved ``key``, or ``None`` when the
key is not an enumerated registry row.

Note this matches the literal rows only; use :func:`is_reserved_meta_key` for the
broader prefix-based reservation test that covers all ``io.modelcontextprotocol/…``
keys. (Appendix C)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.is_reserved_meta_key"></a>

#### is\_reserved\_meta\_key

```python
def is_reserved_meta_key(key: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``key`` is reserved by this document and so MAY appear in
``_meta`` without being treated as an unknown/custom key.

Any key under the reserved ``io.modelcontextprotocol/``/``mcp`` prefix, or one of the
four bare-by-exception keys (``progressToken``, ``traceparent``, ``tracestate``,
``baggage``). (R-AppC-a, AC-46.3)

Reuses ``RESERVED_BARE_KEYS`` (§4.2) and ``is_reserved_meta_key_prefix`` (§2.6.2) so the
reservation surface stays single-sourced. Extension-defined keys outside the reserved
prefix are NOT reserved by this predicate — they are nonetheless permitted in ``_meta``
by the §24/§4 namespacing rules; use :func:`is_meta_key_permitted` to confirm a key MAY
appear at all. (R-AppC-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.is_meta_key_permitted"></a>

#### is\_meta\_key\_permitted

```python
def is_meta_key_permitted(key: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``key`` MAY appear in ``_meta``.

Either because it is a registry-reserved key (see :func:`is_reserved_meta_key`) or
because it is an extension-defined key carried under a valid non-reserved prefix, which
the §24 extension-mechanism and §4 namespacing rules permit. (R-AppC-a, R-AppC-j,
AC-46.3, AC-46.12)

A bare key that is neither reserved-by-exception nor prefixed is NOT permitted (the
spec requires a prefix for any non-reserved key).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.required_client_request_meta_keys"></a>

#### required\_client\_request\_meta\_keys

```python
def required_client_request_meta_keys() -> tuple[str, ...]
```

Return the reserved keys (Appendix C rows) that are REQUIRED on a client request.
(R-AppC-b … d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySide"></a>

#### CapabilitySide

The **Side** column of Appendix D.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CAPABILITY_SIDES"></a>

#### CAPABILITY\_SIDES

The set of legal :data:`CapabilitySide` values (for validation/iteration).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag"></a>

## CapabilitySubFlag Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapabilitySubFlag()
```

A single nested sub-flag of a capability, with its optionality and notes.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag.name"></a>

#### name

The sub-flag member name (for example ``listChanged``, ``form``, ``mimeTypes``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag.requirement"></a>

#### requirement

Requirement level of the sub-flag.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag.gates"></a>

#### gates

One-line statement of what the sub-flag gates or carries.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag.boolean"></a>

#### boolean

When ``True``, the sub-flag is a boolean toggle.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilitySubFlag.deprecated"></a>

#### deprecated

When ``True``, the sub-flag carries Deprecated status.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry"></a>

## CapabilityRegistryEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CapabilityRegistryEntry()
```

One row of Appendix D — a capability defined by this document.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.capability"></a>

#### capability

Capability name (for example ``tools``, ``io.modelcontextprotocol/ui``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.side"></a>

#### side

Which side(s) advertise the capability.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.sub_flags"></a>

#### sub\_flags

Nested members defined for the capability (empty when the value is ``{}``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.defined_in"></a>

#### defined\_in

The section that normatively specifies the capability.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.deprecated"></a>

#### deprecated

When ``True``, the capability as a whole carries Deprecated status.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CapabilityRegistryEntry.extension"></a>

#### extension

When ``True``, the capability is negotiated through the ``extensions`` map.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.CAPABILITY_REGISTRY"></a>

#### CAPABILITY\_REGISTRY

Appendix D — the Capability Registry: every client/server/extension capability defined
by this document, with its side, its sub-flags (and their optionality, boolean-ness,
and deprecation), and its defining section. (Appendix D; R-AppD-a … f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.lookup_capability"></a>

#### lookup\_capability

```python
def lookup_capability(
        capability: str,
        side: CapabilitySide | None = None) -> CapabilityRegistryEntry | None
```

Look up the Appendix D entry for ``capability``.

When the same name is defined on more than one side (``extensions`` is both a client
and a server capability), pass ``side`` to disambiguate; otherwise the first match is
returned. Returns ``None`` when the capability is not in the registry. (Appendix D)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.lookup_capability_sub_flag"></a>

#### lookup\_capability\_sub\_flag

```python
def lookup_capability_sub_flag(
        capability: str,
        sub_flag: str,
        side: CapabilitySide | None = None) -> CapabilitySubFlag | None
```

Return the named sub-flag of a capability, or ``None`` when the capability or the
sub-flag is not defined.

Handy for asserting a sub-flag's optionality, boolean-ness, or deprecation.
(Appendix D)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.UI_HOST_REQUIRED_MIME_TYPE"></a>

#### UI\_HOST\_REQUIRED\_MIME\_TYPE

The MIME type the ``io.modelcontextprotocol/ui`` host value's ``mimeTypes`` array MUST
include. (R-AppD-f, AC-46.18) Pinned as registry DATA; the UI extension (§26) owns the
normative type.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.UiHostValueValidation"></a>

## UiHostValueValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class UiHostValueValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_ui_host_value`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.validate_ui_host_value"></a>

#### validate\_ui\_host\_value

```python
def validate_ui_host_value(value: object) -> UiHostValueValidation
```

Validate the ``io.modelcontextprotocol/ui`` host value against Appendix C/D.

It MUST carry a ``mimeTypes`` array (REQUIRED) that includes
:data:`UI_HOST_REQUIRED_MIME_TYPE`; absence of ``mimeTypes`` is non-conformant.
(R-AppC-h, R-AppD-f, AC-46.10, AC-46.18)

A server *acknowledgement* value (as opposed to the host value) MAY be empty; that case
is the caller's to distinguish — this validator checks the host value, where
``mimeTypes`` is required.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.ToolUiMetaValidation"></a>

## ToolUiMetaValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ToolUiMetaValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_tool_ui_meta_value`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.validate_tool_ui_meta_value"></a>

#### validate\_tool\_ui\_meta\_value

```python
def validate_tool_ui_meta_value(value: object) -> ToolUiMetaValidation
```

Validate a ``Tool`` object's ``_meta.ui`` value against Appendix C.

It MUST be an object with a REQUIRED ``resourceUri`` that is a ``ui://`` URI and an
OPTIONAL ``visibility``; absence of ``resourceUri`` (or a non-``ui://`` value) is
non-conformant. The key is meaningful only when the UI extension is active. (R-AppC-i,
AC-46.11)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.TypeIndexEntry"></a>

## TypeIndexEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TypeIndexEntry()
```

One row of Appendix E — a named wire type declared by this document.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.TypeIndexEntry.type"></a>

#### type

The wire type (interface or type alias) name.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.TypeIndexEntry.defined_in"></a>

#### defined\_in

The section containing the type's full canonical declaration.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.TypeIndexEntry.purpose"></a>

#### purpose

One-line statement of the type's purpose.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.TYPE_REGISTRY"></a>

#### TYPE\_REGISTRY

Appendix E — the Consolidated Type Index: every wire type (interface or type alias)
declared by this document, alphabetically sorted (case-insensitive), each with its
canonical defining section and a one-line purpose. (Appendix E)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.lookup_type"></a>

#### lookup\_type

```python
def lookup_type(type_name: str) -> TypeIndexEntry | None
```

Look up the Appendix E entry for a wire ``type`` name, or ``None`` when the type is
not in the index. (Appendix E)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.APPENDIX_B_RESERVED_CODE_SET"></a>

#### APPENDIX\_B\_RESERVED\_CODE\_SET

The set of reserved error codes the §22 / Appendix B registry pins (the eight codes a
custom code MUST NOT collide with). Surfaced as a convenience set so a caller need not
derive it from ``RESERVED_ERROR_CODES``; the ``-32001`` HeaderMismatch member is the
one that lies inside the ``-32000..-32099`` range. (R-AppB-a, R-AppB-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.registries.is_error_code_defined_by_document"></a>

#### is\_error\_code\_defined\_by\_document

```python
def is_error_code_defined_by_document(code: int) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``code`` is a code the document already defines in Appendix B —
i.e. a code a custom definition MUST avoid.

A ``True`` result means a custom code that equals it is non-conformant. (R-AppB-a,
AC-46.1)

Consults the full ``ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY`` so it catches every listed code (including
the resource-not-found legacy literal), not only the eight in
``RESERVED_ERROR_CODES``. The ``-32001`` HeaderMismatch code is included.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.resources

Resources I — capability, listing, templates & types (§17.1–§17.4).

The discovery surface for resources (server-provided units of context). Fixes the
``resources`` capability (``listChanged``/``subscribe`` sub-flags) + gating, the
``Resource`` and ``ResourceTemplate`` types (with RFC3986 URI and RFC6570 URI-Template
validation), and the paginated/cacheable ``resources/list`` +
``resources/templates/list`` results. Reading is in :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.resources_read`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.RESOURCE_GATED_METHODS"></a>

#### RESOURCE\_GATED\_METHODS

The three requests gated by the ``resources`` capability. (§17.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.ResourcesCapability"></a>

## ResourcesCapability Objects

```python
class ResourcesCapability(McpModel)
```

The ``resources`` capability value (§17.1) — OPTIONAL strict-boolean ``listChanged`` /
``subscribe`` sub-flags; empty ``{}`` valid; extra members pass through.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.is_valid_resources_capability"></a>

#### is\_valid\_resources\_capability

```python
def is_valid_resources_capability(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``resources`` capability: OPTIONAL boolean
``listChanged``/``subscribe``; empty ``{}`` valid. (§17.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.server_declares_resources"></a>

#### server\_declares\_resources

```python
def server_declares_resources(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server declares the ``resources`` capability. (§17.1, R-17.1-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.may_accept_resource_request"></a>

#### may\_accept\_resource\_request

```python
def may_accept_resource_request(method: str, server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a server MAY accept resource request ``method`` — it is gated
AND ``resources`` is declared. (§17.1, R-17.1-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.client_may_issue_resource_request"></a>

#### client\_may\_issue\_resource\_request

```python
def client_may_issue_resource_request(method: str, server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Client-side mirror of :func:`may_accept_resource_request`. (§17.1, R-17.1-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.may_emit_resources_list_changed"></a>

#### may\_emit\_resources\_list\_changed

```python
def may_emit_resources_list_changed(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server MAY emit ``notifications/resources/list_changed``
(needs ``resources`` + ``listChanged``). (§17.1, R-17.1-i/-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.may_emit_resource_updated"></a>

#### may\_emit\_resource\_updated

```python
def may_emit_resource_updated(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server MAY emit ``notifications/resources/updated`` (needs
``resources`` + ``subscribe``). (§17.1, R-17.1-i/-l)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.is_resource_uri"></a>

#### is\_resource\_uri

```python
def is_resource_uri(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a string in URI format [RFC3986] usable as a
concrete ``Resource.uri`` — it carries a conformant scheme and parses as an absolute
URI. A relative reference (no scheme) is rejected. (§17.4, R-17.4-a/-b)

Ports the TS ``isResourceUri`` exactly: a conformant-scheme check followed by an
absolute-URI parse. The WHATWG ``URL`` parser the TS SDK uses rejects a "special"
scheme (``http``/``https``/``ws``/``wss``/``ftp``) with an empty host — e.g.
``"http://"``, ``"https://"``, ``"https:"`` are all invalid — while accepting
``"file:"``, ``"db://"``, ``"urn:isbn:…"`` and any custom scheme. Python's
:func:`urllib.parse.urlsplit` alone is more permissive, so the special-scheme
empty-host cases are checked explicitly to keep byte-for-byte parity with TS.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.is_uri_template"></a>

#### is\_uri\_template

```python
def is_uri_template(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` conforms to the RFC6570 URI Template grammar: literal
characters interspersed with well-formed ``{…}`` expressions. (§17.4, R-17.4-m)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.uri_template_variables"></a>

#### uri\_template\_variables

```python
def uri_template_variables(template: str) -> list[str]
```

Extract the variable names referenced by a template's ``{…}`` expressions, in
first-seen order, modifiers + operator stripped. (§17.4, R-17.4-n)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.ResourceUri"></a>

#### ResourceUri

A concrete resource URI (RFC3986) / a URI Template (RFC6570) — the field-type analogues
of the TS ``isResourceUri`` / ``isUriTemplate`` refinements.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.Resource"></a>

## Resource Objects

```python
class Resource(BaseMetadata)
```

A concrete resource descriptor (§17.4) — the Python analogue of the TS ``ResourceSchema``.

Extends ``BaseMetadata`` with a REQUIRED RFC3986 ``uri`` and the OPTIONAL descriptor
fields. Unknown members pass through (forward-compatible).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.ResourceTemplate"></a>

## ResourceTemplate Objects

```python
class ResourceTemplate(BaseMetadata)
```

A parameterized resource descriptor (§17.4) — the Python analogue of the TS
``ResourceTemplateSchema``. Like ``Resource`` but keyed by a REQUIRED RFC6570
``uriTemplate`` and carrying no ``size``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.is_valid_resource"></a>

#### is\_valid\_resource

```python
def is_valid_resource(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``Resource`` (§17.4): ``BaseMetadata`` + REQUIRED
RFC3986 ``uri``; OPTIONAL ``description``/``mimeType``/``size``/``annotations``/``icons``/``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.is_valid_resource_template"></a>

#### is\_valid\_resource\_template

```python
def is_valid_resource_template(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``ResourceTemplate`` (§17.4): ``BaseMetadata`` +
REQUIRED RFC6570 ``uriTemplate``; same OPTIONAL fields as ``Resource`` minus ``size``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.resources.resource_template_has_no_size"></a>

#### resource\_template\_has\_no\_size

```python
def resource_template_has_no_size(template: dict) -> bool
```
