validation a ``tools/call`` handler uses (backed by the ``jsonschema`` Draft 2020-12
validator, the Python analogue of the TS SDK's Ajv). Calling a tool + ``CallToolResult``
live in :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ToolsCapability"></a>

## ToolsCapability Objects

```python
class ToolsCapability(McpModel)
```

The value of the ``tools`` key in a server's capabilities (§16.1) — an object with an
OPTIONAL strict-boolean ``listChanged`` sub-flag; extra members pass through.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_valid_tools_capability"></a>

#### is\_valid\_tools\_capability

```python
def is_valid_tools_capability(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``ToolsCapability``: an object with OPTIONAL boolean
``listChanged``; extra members tolerated. (§16.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.server_exposes_tools"></a>

#### server\_exposes\_tools

```python
def server_exposes_tools(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server declares the ``tools`` capability. (§16.1, R-16.1-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.may_server_answer_tools_list"></a>

#### may\_server\_answer\_tools\_list

```python
def may_server_answer_tools_list(server_caps: dict,
                                 method: str = TOOLS_LIST_METHOD) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server MAY answer ``tools/list`` / ``tools/call``. (§16.1, R-16.1-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.may_client_send_tools_request"></a>

#### may\_client\_send\_tools\_request

```python
def may_client_send_tools_request(server_caps: dict,
                                  method: str = TOOLS_LIST_METHOD) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client MAY send ``tools/list`` / ``tools/call``. (§16.1, R-16.1-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.may_server_emit_tools_list_changed"></a>

#### may\_server\_emit\_tools\_list\_changed

```python
def may_server_emit_tools_list_changed(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the server MAY emit ``notifications/tools/list_changed``. (R-16.1-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.may_client_expect_tools_list_changed"></a>

#### may\_client\_expect\_tools\_list\_changed

```python
def may_client_expect_tools_list_changed(server_caps: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a client may rely on ``notifications/tools/list_changed``. (R-16.1-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.schema_dialect"></a>

#### schema\_dialect

```python
def schema_dialect(schema: dict) -> str
```

Return the dialect governing a schema: explicit ``$schema`` or the 2020-12 default.
(§16.4(1), R-16.4-a/-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_supported_schema_dialect"></a>

#### is\_supported\_schema\_dialect

```python
def is_supported_schema_dialect(dialect: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``dialect`` is one this implementation can validate. (R-16.4-s/-t)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_in_document_ref"></a>

#### is\_in\_document\_ref

```python
def is_in_document_ref(ref: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a ``$ref``/``$dynamicRef`` resolves WITHIN the document — a
JSON Pointer (``#``, ``#/…``) or a plain-name anchor (```anchor```). (§16.4(5), R-16.4-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.SchemaLimits"></a>

## SchemaLimits Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SchemaLimits()
```

Resource bounds an implementation MAY impose on schema processing. (§16.4(6))

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.has_external_ref"></a>

#### has\_external\_ref

```python
def has_external_ref(node: object,
                     max_depth: int = DEFAULT_SCHEMA_LIMITS.max_depth) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when any ``$ref``/``$dynamicRef`` targets OUTSIDE the document. Pure
structural inspection — never performs I/O, so it cannot trigger an SSRF fetch.
(§16.4(5), R-16.4-f/-g/-r)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.schema_nesting_depth"></a>

#### schema\_nesting\_depth

```python
def schema_nesting_depth(node: object,
                         cap: int = DEFAULT_SCHEMA_LIMITS.max_depth + 1
                         ) -> int
```

Return the max nesting depth (objects + arrays); counting stops at ``cap``. (§16.4(6))

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.UnsupportedDialectError"></a>

## UnsupportedDialectError Objects

```python
class UnsupportedDialectError(Exception)
```

Raised when a tool schema declares a dialect this implementation does not support.
(§16.4(9), R-16.4-t)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ToolSchemaValidation"></a>

## ToolSchemaValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ToolSchemaValidation()
```

Outcome of :func:`validate_tool_schema`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.validate_tool_schema"></a>

#### validate\_tool\_schema

```python
def validate_tool_schema(
        schema: object,
        role: str,
        *,
        limits: SchemaLimits | None = None,
        allow_external_refs: bool = False) -> ToolSchemaValidation
```

Validate a tool's ``inputSchema``/``outputSchema`` against §16.4 (no I/O).

Checks: valid object; supported dialect; depth + node bounds; no external ``$ref``
unless opted in; and for ``role == "input"`` the root ``type`` MUST be ``"object"``.
(R-16.4-d/-f/-g/-k/-l/-n/-s/-t)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.assert_registrable_tool_schema"></a>

#### assert\_registrable\_tool\_schema

```python
def assert_registrable_tool_schema(schema: object,
                                   role: str,
                                   *,
                                   limits: SchemaLimits | None = None,
                                   allow_external_refs: bool = False) -> None
```

Assert a tool schema is safe to register, raising otherwise. (§16.4(7)(9), R-16.4-n/-t)

**Raises**:

- `UnsupportedDialectError`: when the schema declares an unsupported dialect.
- `TypeError`: for any other rejection.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.SchemaValueValidation"></a>

## SchemaValueValidation Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SchemaValueValidation()
```

Outcome of validating a JSON value against a JSON Schema document.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.validate_value_against_schema"></a>

#### validate\_value\_against\_schema

```python
def validate_value_against_schema(schema: object,
                                  value: object) -> SchemaValueValidation
```

Validate a JSON *value* against a JSON Schema *document* (Draft 2020-12).

The machinery a ``tools/call`` handler uses to validate ``arguments`` against
``inputSchema`` and ``structuredContent`` against ``outputSchema``. Returns
``valid=False`` (never raises) when the schema is not a supported 2020-12 object schema
or cannot be compiled (e.g. an unresolvable external ``$ref``). (§16.4, R-16.4-o/-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.validate_tool_arguments"></a>

#### validate\_tool\_arguments

```python
def validate_tool_arguments(tool: dict, args: object) -> SchemaValueValidation
```

Validate a ``tools/call`` ``arguments`` object against the tool's ``inputSchema``. (R-16.4-o)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.validate_tool_structured_content"></a>

#### validate\_tool\_structured\_content

```python
def validate_tool_structured_content(
        tool: dict, structured_content: object) -> SchemaValueValidation
```

Validate a result's ``structuredContent`` against the tool's ``outputSchema``. (R-16.4-p)

When the tool declares no ``outputSchema`` there is nothing to validate.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_conventional_tool_name"></a>

#### is\_conventional\_tool\_name

```python
def is_conventional_tool_name(name: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a tool ``name`` follows conventions: 1–128 chars, only
``A-Z a-z 0-9 _ - .``. (§16.3, R-16.3-b/-c/-d/-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ToolAnnotations"></a>

## ToolAnnotations Objects

```python
class ToolAnnotations(McpModel)
```

Untrusted behavior hints attached to a ``Tool`` (§16.3; semantics in S25). All fields
OPTIONAL; booleans are strict; unknown members pass through. (R-16.3-n)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.Tool"></a>

## Tool Objects

```python
class Tool(BaseMetadata)
```

A single ``Tool`` definition (§16.3) — the Python analogue of the TS ``ToolSchema``.

Extends ``BaseMetadata`` (``name`` REQUIRED, ``title`` OPTIONAL) with the schema and
display fields. ``inputSchema`` is REQUIRED and its root ``type`` MUST be ``"object"``
(R-16.3-k, R-16.4-d); ``outputSchema`` / ``annotations`` / ``icons`` / ``_meta`` are
OPTIONAL. Unknown members pass through (forward-compatible).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_valid_tool"></a>

#### is\_valid\_tool

```python
def is_valid_tool(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``Tool`` (§16.3): ``BaseMetadata`` + REQUIRED
``inputSchema`` whose root ``type`` is ``"object"``; OPTIONAL ``description``,
``outputSchema``, ``annotations``, ``icons``, ``_meta``. (R-16.3-a/-k, R-16.4-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.tool_display_name"></a>

#### tool\_display\_name

```python
def tool_display_name(tool: dict) -> str
```

Resolve a tool's display name: ``title`` → ``annotations.title`` → ``name``. (R-16.3-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.find_duplicate_tool_names"></a>

#### find\_duplicate\_tool\_names

```python
def find_duplicate_tool_names(tools: list[dict]) -> list[str]
```

Return the names occurring more than once across ``tools``. (R-16.3-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.disambiguate_tool_name"></a>

#### disambiguate\_tool\_name

```python
def disambiguate_tool_name(server_id: str,
                           name: str,
                           separator: str = ".") -> str
```

Prefix a tool ``name`` with a server identifier to disambiguate a collision. (R-16.3-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ListToolsResult"></a>

## ListToolsResult Objects

```python
class ListToolsResult(McpModel)
```

The result of ``tools/list`` (§16.2) — the Python analogue of the TS
``ListToolsResultSchema``.

Simultaneously a paginated result (``nextCursor``, §12) and a cacheable result
(``ttlMs`` / ``cacheScope``, §13) wrapping the REQUIRED page of ``Tool`` definitions.
``resultType`` is fixed to ``"complete"`` (R-16.2-m).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_valid_list_tools_result"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_tools\_result

```python
def is_valid_list_tools_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``ListToolsResult`` (§16.2): ``resultType``
``"complete"``, a ``tools`` list of valid Tools, OPTIONAL opaque ``nextCursor``,
REQUIRED non-negative ``ttlMs`` and ``cacheScope``. (R-16.2-b/-c/-g/-j/-m)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ListToolsResultConfig"></a>

## ListToolsResultConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ListToolsResultConfig()
```

The server-supplied inputs to a ``ListToolsResult``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.build_list_tools_result"></a>

#### build\_list\_tools\_result

```python
def build_list_tools_result(config: ListToolsResultConfig) -> dict
```

Build a ``ListToolsResult`` with ``resultType: "complete"``; optional fields only

when supplied. (§16.2)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``ttl_ms`` is negative or not an integer. (R-16.2-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.ListToolsRequestParams"></a>

## ListToolsRequestParams Objects

```python
class ListToolsRequestParams(McpModel)
```

The ``params`` of a ``tools/list`` request (§16.2): OPTIONAL opaque ``cursor`` and
OPTIONAL ``_meta``; a first-page request MAY omit both. (R-16.2-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_valid_list_tools_request_params"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_tools\_request\_params

```python
def is_valid_list_tools_request_params(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a valid ``tools/list`` ``params`` object (§16.2): OPTIONAL opaque
string ``cursor`` and OPTIONAL ``_meta`` map; extra members tolerated. (R-16.2-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.is_valid_list_tools_request"></a>

#### is\_valid\_list\_tools\_request

```python
def is_valid_list_tools_request(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``tools/list`` request envelope (§16.2): ``jsonrpc``
``"2.0"``, a request ``id``, ``method`` ``"tools/list"``, and OPTIONAL ``params`` (a valid
:func:`is_valid_list_tools_request_params`). Omitting ``params`` entirely requests the
first page. (R-16.2-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools.build_list_tools_request"></a>

#### build\_list\_tools\_request

```python
def build_list_tools_request(id_: str | int,
                             cursor: str | None = None,
                             extra_meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a ``tools/list`` request. A supplied ``cursor`` is passed VERBATIM (opaque);
omitting it requests the first page. (§16.2, R-16.2-a/-d/-e/-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.tools\_call

Tools II — calling, errors, annotations & change notifications (§16.5–§16.9).

The runtime half of MCP tools on top of :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.tools`. Defines the
``tools/call`` request (incl. the multi-round-trip retry fields), the ``CallToolResult``
(with the ``isError`` model), the two-layer error split (a tool-execution failure is a
*successful* result with ``isError: true``; a dispatch failure is a JSON-RPC ``-32602``),
the ``dispatchToolCall`` decision, tool annotations, and the list-changed notification.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.CallToolRequestParams"></a>

## CallToolRequestParams Objects

```python
class CallToolRequestParams(McpModel)
```

The ``params`` of a ``tools/call`` request (§16.5) — the Python analogue of the TS
``CallToolRequestParamsSchema``.

``name`` is the REQUIRED tool name (a string); ``arguments`` and ``inputResponses`` are
OPTIONAL objects; ``requestState`` is an OPTIONAL opaque string echoed on retry; ``_meta``
is an OPTIONAL reserved metadata object. Unknown members pass through
(``extra="allow"`` ≙ ``.passthrough()``). A non-object ``arguments``/``inputResponses``,
a non-string ``requestState`` or ``name``, or a non-object ``_meta`` fail validation.
(R-16.5-a, R-16.5-c, R-16.5-f, R-16.5-h, R-16.5-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.is_call_tool_request"></a>

#### is\_call\_tool\_request

```python
def is_call_tool_request(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed ``tools/call`` request: the JSON-RPC envelope plus a
``params`` object that validates as :class:`CallToolRequestParams`. (§16.5, R-16.5-a)

Mirrors the TS ``CallToolRequestSchema.safeParse``: beyond a string ``params.name`` the
request-shape contract also rejects a non-object ``arguments``/``inputResponses``, a
non-string ``requestState``, and a non-object ``_meta``. (Both a malformed and a
well-formed-but-unknown-tool call still converge on ``-32602`` at dispatch, but the
shape predicate now matches TS rather than only checking ``name``.)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.resolve_call_tool_arguments"></a>

#### resolve\_call\_tool\_arguments

```python
def resolve_call_tool_arguments(params: dict) -> dict
```

Resolve the effective arguments: the supplied object, or ``{}`` when omitted.
A server MUST treat omitted ``arguments`` as ``{}``. (§16.5, R-16.5-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.CallToolRequestConfig"></a>

## CallToolRequestConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CallToolRequestConfig()
```

Caller-supplied inputs to a first-issue ``tools/call``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_call_tool_request"></a>

#### build\_call\_tool\_request

```python
def build_call_tool_request(id_: str | int,
                            config: CallToolRequestConfig) -> dict
```

Build a first-issue ``tools/call`` request; ``arguments``/``_meta`` only when supplied
(the server applies the omitted-arguments default). (§16.5)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.CallToolRetryConfig"></a>

## CallToolRetryConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CallToolRetryConfig()
```

Caller-supplied inputs to a retry of a previously ``input_required`` call.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_call_tool_retry_request"></a>

#### build\_call\_tool\_retry\_request

```python
def build_call_tool_retry_request(initial_id: str | int, retry_id: str | int,
                                  config: CallToolRetryConfig) -> dict
```

Build a retry ``tools/call`` after an ``input_required`` result, echoing

``request_state`` verbatim and supplying ``inputResponses``. (§16.5, S17)

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``retry_id`` equals ``initial_id`` — a retry MUST use a fresh
JSON-RPC id. (R-16.5-u)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.CallToolResult"></a>

## CallToolResult Objects

```python
class CallToolResult(McpModel)
```

A completed ``tools/call`` result (§16.5) — the Python analogue of the TS
``CallToolResultSchema`` (its ``"complete"`` variant).

``content`` (REQUIRED) is the unstructured ``ContentBlock`` array; ``structuredContent``
(OPTIONAL, any JSON value); ``isError`` (OPTIONAL strict bool; absent ⇒ success);
``resultType`` is fixed to ``"complete"`` (the ``"input_required"`` variant is the S17
``InputRequiredResult``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.is_call_tool_result"></a>

#### is\_call\_tool\_result

```python
def is_call_tool_result(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed (completed) ``CallToolResult`` (§16.5): ``resultType``
``"complete"``, a ``content`` list of valid blocks, OPTIONAL ``isError`` bool, ``_meta``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.is_call_tool_error"></a>

#### is\_call\_tool\_error

```python
def is_call_tool_error(result: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the result ended in a tool-execution error (``isError == True``;
absent ⇒ success). (§16.5/§16.6, R-16.5-q, R-16.6-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.is_structured_content_present"></a>

#### is\_structured\_content\_present

```python
def is_structured_content_present(result: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``structuredContent`` is present (an explicit ``null`` counts;
an omitted key does not). (R-16.5-n)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.structured_content_text_fallback"></a>

#### structured\_content\_text\_fallback

```python
def structured_content_text_fallback(structured_content: object) -> dict
```

Serialize a structured value to a ``text`` content block — the SHOULD fallback for
clients that do not consume structured content. (§16.5, R-16.5-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.CallToolResultConfig"></a>

## CallToolResultConfig Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CallToolResultConfig()
```

Server-supplied inputs to a (non-error) ``CallToolResult``.

Leave ``structured_content`` as the default sentinel to omit it; pass any value
(including ``None``) to include it.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_call_tool_result"></a>

#### build\_call\_tool\_result

```python
def build_call_tool_result(config: CallToolResultConfig) -> dict
```

Build a completed ``CallToolResult`` (``resultType: "complete"``); optional fields only
when supplied (an explicit ``structured_content=None`` survives). (§16.5)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_output_schema_result"></a>

#### build\_output\_schema\_result

```python
def build_output_schema_result(structured_content: object,
                               extra_content: list | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a successful result for a tool with an ``outputSchema``: ``structuredContent`` +
a prepended JSON-text fallback. (§16.5, R-16.5-o/-p)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_tool_execution_error"></a>

#### build\_tool\_execution\_error

```python
def build_tool_execution_error(message: str,
                               *,
                               content: list | None = None,
                               structured_content: object = _OMITTED,
                               meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a tool-execution-error result — a successful ``CallToolResult`` with
``isError: true`` (NOT a JSON-RPC error), so the model can observe and self-correct.
(§16.6, R-16.6-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_unknown_tool_error"></a>

#### build\_unknown\_tool\_error

```python
def build_unknown_tool_error(name: str) -> dict
```

Build the JSON-RPC ``-32602`` error for an UNKNOWN tool name (never a CallToolResult).
(§16.6, R-16.5-b, R-16.6-d/-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_invalid_arguments_error"></a>

#### build\_invalid\_arguments\_error

```python
def build_invalid_arguments_error(name: str,
                                  errors: list[str] | None = None) -> dict
```

Build the JSON-RPC ``-32602`` error for arguments that fail the tool's ``inputSchema``;
the tool MUST NOT be invoked. (§16.6, R-16.5-d, R-16.6-d/-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.ToolDispatch"></a>

## ToolDispatch Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ToolDispatch()
```

Outcome of :func:`dispatch_tool_call`.

``dispatched`` with ``tool`` + ``arguments`` on success; otherwise ``error`` carries the
JSON-RPC protocol error to return. The two layers §16.6 keeps strictly distinct.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.dispatch_tool_call"></a>

#### dispatch\_tool\_call

```python
def dispatch_tool_call(params: dict,
                       exposed_tools: list[dict]) -> ToolDispatch
```

Perform the §16.6 dispatch decision (no raising): unknown tool → ``-32602``; arguments
failing the tool's ``inputSchema`` → ``-32602`` (tool NOT invoked); otherwise dispatched
with resolved arguments. Tool names match case-sensitively. (§16.6, R-16.6-a/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.TOOL_ANNOTATION_DEFAULTS"></a>

#### TOOL\_ANNOTATION\_DEFAULTS

The §16.7 default values for the four boolean annotation hints. (R-16.7-b–R-16.7-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.resolve_tool_annotation_hints"></a>

#### resolve\_tool\_annotation\_hints

```python
def resolve_tool_annotation_hints(annotations: dict | None) -> dict
```

Resolve the four boolean ``ToolAnnotations`` hints, applying the §16.7 defaults for
absent fields. ``destructiveHint``/``idempotentHint`` are meaningful only when
``readOnlyHint`` is ``False``. (R-16.7-b–R-16.7-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.may_trust_tool_annotations"></a>

#### may\_trust\_tool\_annotations

```python
def may_trust_tool_annotations(server_is_trusted: bool = False) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` ONLY when the server is explicitly trusted — annotations are untrusted
hints, so safety decisions fail closed for any untrusted server. (§16.7, R-16.7-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.is_tool_list_changed_notification"></a>

#### is\_tool\_list\_changed\_notification

```python
def is_tool_list_changed_notification(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for a well-formed list-changed notification (no ``id``). (§16.8)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.build_tool_list_changed_notification"></a>

#### build\_tool\_list\_changed\_notification

```python
def build_tool_list_changed_notification(meta: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a ``notifications/tools/list_changed`` notification; ``params`` only when ``_meta``
is supplied. (§16.8, R-16.8-a/-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.tools_call.react_to_tool_list_changed"></a>

#### react\_to\_tool\_list\_changed

```python
def react_to_tool_list_changed() -> dict
```

The prescribed client reaction: invalidate the cached tool list (SHOULD) and MAY
re-list. (§16.8, R-16.8-c/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.ui

Interactive UI Extension I: Negotiation, UI Declaration & UI Resource (§26.1–§26.4).

The server-facing, *static* half of the OPTIONAL Interactive User-Interface ("apps")
extension: how the extension is identified and negotiated, how a server DECLARES that
one of its tools has an associated interactive HTML interface (``_meta.ui`` ⇒ the
``{resourceUri, visibility?}`` shape validated by :func:`is_tool_ui_meta`), and how
that interface is served as an ordinary MCP resource under the ``ui://`` scheme with
the verbatim ``text/html;profile=mcp-app`` MIME type (the UI resource and its
:func:`is_resource_ui_meta` presentation/security hints).

The extension is an instance of the general Extension Mechanism (§24): the identifier
:data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` is an ordinary key in the ``extensions`` capability map,
negotiated by intersection, and ``_meta.ui`` is the extension's reserved tool metadata
key. The TypeScript SDK reuses dedicated S38/S11 extension modules for that machinery;
the Python port has no such modules yet, so the small subset of extension primitives
this layer needs (advertised/active/intersection/may-emit) is reproduced here as
private helpers (:func:`_is_extension_settings`, :func:`_get_extension_settings`,
:func:`_is_extension_advertised`, :func:`_is_extension_active`, :func:`_may_emit_surface`)
with the EXACT semantics of their TS counterparts (R-6.5-h/-j/-l, R-24.3, R-24.4).

The UI rendering itself, the sandbox/CSP enforcement, the host-provided message
channel, and consent mediation are HOST responsibilities and are NOT implemented by a
server SDK — a conforming server SDK MUST be implementable with no
rendering/browser/UI-toolkit dependency (R-26.1-i). This module therefore models the
host obligations declaratively (documented constants and predicates a host can consult)
but never renders anything.

This is the protocol-level validation layer; the server shaping helpers (which build
``ui://`` resource blocks and launcher tool results) live in :mod:`stackific.mcp.server.ui`. The
canonical constants are defined HERE; the server module's matching values are aliases
of the same strings.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_EXTENSION_ID"></a>

#### UI\_EXTENSION\_ID

The Interactive UI ("apps") extension identifier: the exact, opaque,
case-sensitive string used as a key in the ``extensions`` capability map.
A receiver MUST treat this as an opaque, case-sensitive string — compare with
:func:`extension_ids_match`, never with case folding, so ``IO.ModelContextProtocol/UI``
does NOT match. (§26.2, R-26.2-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_MIME_TYPE"></a>

#### UI\_MIME\_TYPE

The UI resource MIME type, reproduced verbatim and case-sensitively, including the
``;profile=mcp-app`` parameter and the ABSENCE of surrounding whitespace. A host that
supports the extension MUST include this exact string in its advertised ``mimeTypes``;
a UI resource MUST be served with this exact type. ``"text/html; profile=mcp-app"``
(extra space) and ``"TEXT/HTML;PROFILE=MCP-APP"`` (wrong case) do NOT satisfy it.
(§26.2 / §26.4, R-26.2-e, R-26.4-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_URI_SCHEME"></a>

#### UI\_URI\_SCHEME

The ``ui://`` URI scheme prefix designating an MCP UI resource. The host MUST treat
the whole URI as opaque and MUST NOT derive a network origin from it.
(§26.4, R-26.4-b, R-26.4-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.extension_ids_match"></a>

#### extension\_ids\_match

```python
def extension_ids_match(a: str, b: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when two extension identifiers are equal, compared verbatim and
case-sensitively (never case-folded). (§26.2, R-26.2-b; §24)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_mime_type"></a>

#### is\_ui\_mime\_type

```python
def is_ui_mime_type(mime_type: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``mime_type`` is exactly :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE` — matched
verbatim and case-sensitively, with no whitespace tolerance. (R-26.2-e, R-26.4-d)

This single gate backs both "the host advertised the required type" and "the resource
was served with the required type": both demand the byte-exact string, so trimming or
lower-casing would be non-conformant.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_resource_uri"></a>

#### is\_ui\_resource\_uri

```python
def is_ui_resource_uri(uri: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``uri`` is a ``ui://``-scheme URI string. (§26.4, R-26.3-b,
R-26.4-b, R-26.4-c)

The authority and path after ``ui://`` are server-defined and opaque; this only
checks the scheme — it deliberately parses no structure, because the host MUST treat
the whole URI as an opaque identifier and derive no network origin from it.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_RESPONSIBILITIES"></a>

#### UI\_RESPONSIBILITIES

The discrete responsibilities the apps extension assigns, each fixed to a single
role. (§26.1, R-26.1-b..h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_RESPONSIBILITY_OWNER"></a>

#### UI\_RESPONSIBILITY\_OWNER

The fixed, normative assignment of each responsibility to the role that owns it. The
server (and server-side SDK) is RESPONSIBLE only for declaring the association and
serving the resource; everything to do with rendering, isolation, policy enforcement,
the channel, and consent is the host's. (§26.1, R-26.1-b..h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.ui_responsibility_owner"></a>

#### ui\_responsibility\_owner

```python
def ui_responsibility_owner(responsibility: str) -> str
```

Return the role (``"server"`` / ``"host"``) that owns ``responsibility``. (§26.1)

**Raises**:

- `KeyError`: when ``responsibility`` is not a recognized responsibility.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_server_responsibility"></a>

#### is\_server\_responsibility

```python
def is_server_responsibility(responsibility: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``responsibility`` belongs to the server — one of the only two
server obligations, declaring ``_meta.ui`` and serving the ``ui://`` resource.
(R-26.1-b, R-26.1-c)

Every other responsibility (render, sandbox, enforce CSP/permissions, run the channel,
mediate consent) returns ``False``: a conforming server SDK does NOT carry them and
MUST be implementable with no rendering/browser/UI-toolkit dependency. (R-26.1-d, R-26.1-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UiHostExtensionCapability"></a>

## UiHostExtensionCapability Objects

```python
class UiHostExtensionCapability(McpModel)
```

The host's advertised apps-extension capability value (§26.2): a ``mimeTypes`` array of
strings; extra members pass through. (R-26.2-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_host_extension_capability"></a>

#### is\_ui\_host\_extension\_capability

```python
def is_ui_host_extension_capability(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed ``UiHostExtensionCapability`` — an
object carrying a ``mimeTypes`` array of strings. (§26.2, R-26.2-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.capability_renders_ui"></a>

#### capability\_renders\_ui

```python
def capability_renders_ui(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when an advertised host capability value enables UI rendering: it is
a well-formed ``UiHostExtensionCapability`` AND its ``mimeTypes`` contains the verbatim
:data:`UI_MIME_TYPE`. (R-26.2-d, R-26.2-e)

A capability whose ``mimeTypes`` carries only ``"text/html; profile=mcp-app"`` (extra
whitespace) or ``"TEXT/HTML;PROFILE=MCP-APP"`` (wrong case) returns ``False``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.build_ui_host_extension_capability"></a>

#### build\_ui\_host\_extension\_capability

```python
def build_ui_host_extension_capability(additional_mime_types: Iterable[str] = (
)) -> dict
```

Build a conformant ``UiHostExtensionCapability`` for a host that supports UI
rendering. :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE` is always included (deduplicated) so the result
satisfies R-26.2-e; additional renderable MIME types MAY be supplied and are appended
in order. (§26.2, R-26.2-d, R-26.2-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.get_ui_host_capability"></a>

#### get\_ui\_host\_capability

```python
def get_ui_host_capability(extensions_map: object) -> dict | None
```

Return the ``UiHostExtensionCapability`` a host advertised under
:data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` in an ``extensions`` map (raw), or ``None`` when the extension
is not validly advertised or its value is not a well-formed capability.
(§26.2, R-26.2-c, R-26.2-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.host_advertises_ui_rendering"></a>

#### host\_advertises\_ui\_rendering

```python
def host_advertises_ui_rendering(extensions_map: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a host's ``extensions`` map advertises the apps extension in a
way that enables UI rendering: the :data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` key is present with a
capability whose ``mimeTypes`` includes the verbatim :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE`.
(§26.2, R-26.2-c, R-26.2-d, R-26.2-e)

This is the predicate behind the server's two prohibitions: a server MUST NOT declare
UI associations (R-26.2-f) and MUST NOT expect any UI resource to be rendered
(R-26.2-g) unless this returns ``True``. See :func:`may_server_declare_ui` /
:func:`may_server_expect_rendering`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.request_advertises_ui_rendering"></a>

#### request\_advertises\_ui\_rendering

```python
def request_advertises_ui_rendering(request_meta: object) -> bool
```

Read the host's advertised ``extensions`` map from a single request's ``_meta`` (the
map nested under ``io.modelcontextprotocol/clientCapabilities.extensions``) and report
whether it advertises UI rendering with the required MIME type. (§26.2, R-26.2-c)

A host that supports rendering UIs MUST advertise the extension in the ``_meta`` of
EVERY request (R-26.2-c); the stateless model means each request is judged on its own
``_meta``. A request whose ``_meta`` omits the advertisement — or omits
``clientCapabilities`` entirely — yields ``False``, and the server treats that request
as if the extension were inactive (R-26.2-i).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.may_server_declare_ui"></a>

#### may\_server\_declare\_ui

```python
def may_server_declare_ui(host_extensions_map: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a server MAY declare UI associations on its tools — only when
the host has advertised the extension with a ``mimeTypes`` array that includes the
verbatim :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE`. A server MUST NOT declare otherwise. (§26.2, R-26.2-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.may_server_expect_rendering"></a>

#### may\_server\_expect\_rendering

```python
def may_server_expect_rendering(host_extensions_map: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a server MAY expect a UI resource to be rendered — only when the
host has advertised the extension with the required :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE`. A server MUST
NOT expect rendering otherwise. (§26.2, R-26.2-g)

Same gate as :func:`may_server_declare_ui`; named separately so each prohibition
(declare vs expect-rendering) reads clearly at the call site.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_extension_active"></a>

#### is\_ui\_extension\_active

```python
def is_ui_extension_active(client_extensions: object,
                           server_extensions: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the apps extension is ACTIVE between client and server — both
validly advertise :data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` in their ``extensions`` maps. (§26.2, R-26.2-a)

Mere presence of the key on one side does not activate the extension; the receiver
computes the intersection. When inactive, the host treats a tool carrying ``_meta.ui``
as a normal tool and ignores the UI key. (R-26.2-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.may_emit_ui_surface"></a>

#### may\_emit\_ui\_surface

```python
def may_emit_ui_surface(active_set: Iterable[str]) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when the apps extension is in ``active_set`` and the server MAY
therefore emit its surface (the ``_meta.ui`` key, the ``ui://`` resource) for this
interaction. (§26.2, R-26.2-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_server_ui_acknowledgement"></a>

#### is\_server\_ui\_acknowledgement

```python
def is_server_ui_acknowledgement(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a valid ``ServerUiAcknowledgement`` — any object,
which MAY be empty (``{}``). Presence of the key is what signals acknowledgement.
(§26.2, R-26.2-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.build_server_ui_acknowledgement"></a>

#### build\_server\_ui\_acknowledgement

```python
def build_server_ui_acknowledgement() -> dict
```

Build the ``capabilities.extensions`` fragment a server includes in its
``server/discover`` result to acknowledge the apps extension: a single
:data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` key mapped to an empty object. (§26.2, R-26.2-j)

Acknowledgement is OPTIONAL (MAY); a server merges this fragment into the
``extensions`` map of its result capabilities when it chooses to acknowledge.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.server_acknowledges_ui"></a>

#### server\_acknowledges\_ui

```python
def server_acknowledges_ui(server_extensions_map: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a server's ``server/discover`` result ``capabilities.extensions``
map acknowledges the apps extension — the :data:`UI_EXTENSION_ID` key is present with a
(possibly empty) object value. (§26.2, R-26.2-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.TOOL_UI_META_KEY"></a>

#### TOOL\_UI\_META\_KEY

The reserved nested key under a tool's ``_meta`` that carries the UI declaration:
``ui``, giving the path ``_meta.ui``. (§26.3)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_VISIBILITY_VALUES"></a>

#### UI\_VISIBILITY\_VALUES

The exact visibility enum strings: which actor may invoke a tool. ``"model"`` —
callable by the model/agent via ordinary tool-calling (§16); ``"app"`` — callable by
the rendered UI over the channel (§26.5). (§26.3, R-26.3-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.DEFAULT_UI_VISIBILITY"></a>

#### DEFAULT\_UI\_VISIBILITY

The effective ``visibility`` when ``_meta.ui.visibility`` is omitted: both actors may
invoke the tool. (§26.3, R-26.3-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_visibility"></a>

#### is\_ui\_visibility

```python
def is_ui_visibility(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is one of the exact visibility enum strings
(``"model"`` / ``"app"``), matched case-sensitively. (R-26.3-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.ToolUiMeta"></a>

## ToolUiMeta Objects

```python
class ToolUiMeta(McpModel)
```

The ``_meta.ui`` declaration on a tool (§26.3) — a ``ui://`` ``resourceUri`` and an
OPTIONAL ``visibility`` array of ``"model"``/``"app"`` (omitted ⇒ both). (R-26.3-a/-b/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_tool_ui_meta"></a>

#### is\_tool\_ui\_meta

```python
def is_tool_ui_meta(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed ``ToolUiMeta`` — the object at a
tool's ``_meta.ui`` declaring its associated interactive UI. (§26.3, R-26.3-a/-b/-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.get_tool_ui_meta"></a>

#### get\_tool\_ui\_meta

```python
def get_tool_ui_meta(tool: object) -> dict | None
```

Extract the ``ToolUiMeta`` from a tool — i.e. parse ``tool._meta.ui`` — returning
``None`` when there is no ``_meta``, no ``ui`` key, or the value is not a well-formed
declaration. (§26.3)

This does NOT gate on negotiation: a receiver that has not negotiated the extension
MUST ignore the key (R-26.3-g) — use :func:`read_tool_ui_meta` for the
negotiation-aware read.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.read_tool_ui_meta"></a>

#### read\_tool\_ui\_meta

```python
def read_tool_ui_meta(tool: object, active_set: Iterable[str]) -> dict | None
```

Read a tool's UI declaration ONLY when the extension is active for the interaction;
return ``None`` when inactive, modeling "a receiver that does not negotiate this
extension MUST ignore the ``_meta.ui`` key". (§26.3, R-26.3-g, R-26.2-i)

When inactive the tool is treated as a normal tool and the key is ignored — its
presence MUST NOT change the behavior of an ordinary ``tools/call`` (R-26.3-h).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.effective_visibility"></a>

#### effective\_visibility

```python
def effective_visibility(meta: dict) -> tuple[str, ...]
```

Return the EFFECTIVE visibility of a UI declaration: the declared ``visibility``
array when present, otherwise the default ``("model", "app")``. (§26.3, R-26.3-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_app_invokable"></a>

#### is\_app\_invokable

```python
def is_app_invokable(meta: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a tool's effective visibility includes ``"app"`` — i.e. the
rendered UI MAY invoke it over the channel. A host SHOULD reject a UI-originated
``tools/call`` for a tool whose effective ``visibility`` does NOT include ``"app"``.
(§26.3, R-26.3-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.host_should_reject_ui_originated_call"></a>

#### host\_should\_reject\_ui\_originated\_call

```python
def host_should_reject_ui_originated_call(meta: dict | None) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a host SHOULD REJECT a ``tools/call`` that originates from a
rendered UI, given the tool's UI declaration: rejected exactly when the tool's
effective visibility excludes ``"app"``. (§26.3, R-26.3-e)

A tool with no UI declaration (``None``) was not exposed to the UI at all; a
UI-originated call for it is likewise rejected.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_visible_to_model"></a>

#### is\_visible\_to\_model

```python
def is_visible_to_model(meta: dict) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a tool's effective visibility includes ``"model"`` — i.e. it
appears in the model's tool list and is callable via ordinary tool-calling. A tool
with ``visibility`` ``["app"]`` is callable ONLY by the UI and is HIDDEN from the
model's tool list, so this returns ``False``. (§26.3, R-26.3-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.tools_visible_to_model"></a>

#### tools\_visible\_to\_model

```python
def tools_visible_to_model(tools: Iterable, active_set: Iterable[str]) -> list
```

Filter tools to those visible to the model, applying the §26.3 hide rule: a tool
whose effective UI visibility is ``["app"]``-only is omitted from the model's tool
list. (§26.3, R-26.3-f)

The extension must be active for the rule to apply (R-26.3-g): when inactive,
``_meta.ui`` is ignored and every tool is treated as ordinary and model-visible. A
tool with no UI declaration is always model-visible.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_CSP_DIRECTIVES"></a>

#### UI\_CSP\_DIRECTIVES

The four CSP descriptor members, in spec order. (§26.4, R-26.4-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.DENY_BY_DEFAULT_CSP"></a>

#### DENY\_BY\_DEFAULT\_CSP

The deny-by-default CSP a host MUST apply when a UI resource omits ``csp``: every
directive is an empty origin list, so every origin is blocked. (§26.4, R-26.4-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UiContentSecurityPolicy"></a>

## UiContentSecurityPolicy Objects

```python
class UiContentSecurityPolicy(McpModel)
```

A ``UiContentSecurityPolicy`` (§26.4): OPTIONAL directive members, each an array of
origin strings; extra members pass through. (R-26.4-f, R-26.4-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_content_security_policy"></a>

#### is\_ui\_content\_security\_policy

```python
def is_ui_content_security_policy(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed ``UiContentSecurityPolicy`` — an
object whose present directive members are each arrays of origin strings. (§26.4, R-26.4-f/-g)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.csp_allows_origin"></a>

#### csp\_allows\_origin

```python
def csp_allows_origin(csp: dict | None, directive: str, origin: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``origin`` is ALLOWED for the given CSP ``directive`` of a
``csp`` descriptor — it is explicitly listed in that member. An origin not listed
(including when the member is absent) MUST be blocked. (§26.4, R-26.4-g)

When ``csp`` is ``None`` (omitted), deny-by-default applies and this always returns
``False`` (R-26.4-h).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.resolve_csp"></a>

#### resolve\_csp

```python
def resolve_csp(csp: dict | None) -> dict
```

Resolve the CSP a host applies for a UI resource: the declared ``csp`` when present,
otherwise the restrictive :data:`DENY_BY_DEFAULT_CSP` (deny-by-default). (§26.4, R-26.4-h)

The host MUST apply a restrictive policy CONSTRAINED by the declared descriptor — it
never grants an origin the descriptor did not list — so a present ``csp`` is returned
as-is for the host to constrain by (R-26.4-o). An absent ``csp`` yields the all-empty
deny-by-default policy.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UI_PERMISSION_NAMES"></a>

#### UI\_PERMISSION\_NAMES

The four sandbox capability names a UI MAY request, in spec order. (§26.4, R-26.4-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.UiPermissions"></a>

## UiPermissions Objects

```python
class UiPermissions(McpModel)
```

A ``UiPermissions`` object (§26.4): OPTIONAL capability members, each an object ``{}``
whose presence requests that sandbox capability; extra members pass through. (R-26.4-i/-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_permissions"></a>

#### is\_ui\_permissions

```python
def is_ui_permissions(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed ``UiPermissions`` — an object whose
present capability members are each objects ``{}``. (§26.4, R-26.4-i, R-26.4-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.permission_requested"></a>

#### permission\_requested

```python
def permission_requested(permissions: dict | None, name: str) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a UI resource's ``permissions`` REQUESTS the named sandbox
capability — i.e. the member is present. Absence means the capability is not requested,
and the host MUST NOT grant it. (§26.4, R-26.4-i, R-26.4-j)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.requested_permissions"></a>

#### requested\_permissions

```python
def requested_permissions(permissions: dict | None) -> list[str]
```

Return the set of sandbox capabilities a UI resource requests, as the subset of
:data:`UI_PERMISSION_NAMES` present in ``permissions``. The host MUST NOT grant any
capability outside this set (R-26.4-j) and MAY decline any within it (R-26.4-k).
(§26.4, R-26.4-i)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.may_grant_permission"></a>

#### may\_grant\_permission

```python
def may_grant_permission(permissions: dict | None,
                         name: str,
                         host_declines: bool = False) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when a host MAY grant the named sandbox capability for a UI resource:
ONLY when it was requested (the host MUST NOT grant an unrequested capability) AND the
host did not decline it (the host MAY decline a requested one). (§26.4, R-26.4-j, R-26.4-k)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.ResourceUiMeta"></a>

## ResourceUiMeta Objects

```python
class ResourceUiMeta(McpModel)
```

The optional presentation/security hints on a UI resource ``contents`` entry's
``_meta.ui`` (§26.4): all OPTIONAL — ``csp``, ``permissions``, ``domain`` (string),
``prefersBorder`` (boolean). Extra members pass through. (R-26.4-e/-f/-i/-l/-m)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_resource_ui_meta"></a>

#### is\_resource\_ui\_meta

```python
def is_resource_ui_meta(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed ``ResourceUiMeta`` — the optional
presentation and security hints carried on a UI resource's ``contents`` entry under its
own ``_meta.ui``. (§26.4, R-26.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_resource_contents"></a>

#### is\_ui\_resource\_contents

```python
def is_ui_resource_contents(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a well-formed UI resource ``contents`` entry: an
ordinary ``ResourceContents`` (§14.5 — ``text`` or ``blob``, mutually exclusive) whose
``mimeType`` is the verbatim :data:`UI_MIME_TYPE`, OPTIONALLY carrying ``ResourceUiMeta``
hints under ``_meta.ui``. (§26.4, R-26.4-d, R-26.4-e)

The base shape and the text/blob exclusivity come from :func:`is_valid_resource_contents`;
this narrows the ``mimeType`` to the exact UI type (so any other MIME type is rejected)
and validates the nested ``_meta.ui`` hint object when present.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.get_resource_ui_meta"></a>

#### get\_resource\_ui\_meta

```python
def get_resource_ui_meta(contents: object) -> dict | None
```

Extract the ``ResourceUiMeta`` hints from a UI resource ``contents`` entry — i.e.
parse ``contents._meta.ui`` — returning ``None`` when there are no hints or they are
malformed. When present, these hints take effect for rendering. (§26.4, R-26.4-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.build_ui_resource_contents"></a>

#### build\_ui\_resource\_contents

```python
def build_ui_resource_contents(uri: str,
                               *,
                               text: str | None = None,
                               blob: str | None = None,
                               ui: dict | None = None) -> dict
```

Build a UI resource ``contents`` entry: the ``ui://`` ``uri``, the verbatim

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``uri`` is not a ``ui://`` URI (R-26.4-b), or when neither or
both of ``text`` and ``blob`` are supplied (R-14.5-h).

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.build_ui_resource_read_result"></a>

#### build\_ui\_resource\_read\_result

```python
def build_ui_resource_read_result(contents: dict, *, ttl_ms: int,
                                  cache_scope: str) -> dict
```

Build the result object a server returns from ``resources/read`` for a UI resource:

a complete, cacheable result carrying the single UI ``contents`` entry. (§26.4)

The result mirrors the §27 ``ReadResourceResult`` shape used in the §26.4 wire
example: ``resultType: "complete"``, a ``contents`` array, and the REQUIRED
``ttlMs`` / ``cacheScope`` cache fields.

**Raises**:

- `ValueError`: when ``ttl_ms`` is not a non-negative integer (R-13), or when
``cache_scope`` is not ``"public"`` / ``"private"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui.ui_resource_read_uri"></a>

#### ui\_resource\_read\_uri

```python
def ui_resource_read_uri(meta: dict | None) -> str | None
```

Return the ``ui://`` URI to use in a ``resources/read`` request for a tool's UI
resource: the EXACT ``resourceUri`` from the tool's ``_meta.ui``, treated as an opaque
identifier. The host issues ``resources/read`` for this exact string and MUST NOT
derive a network origin from it. (§26.4, R-26.3-c, R-26.4-b, R-26.4-c)

Returns ``None`` when ``meta`` is ``None`` or carries no ``resourceUri``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host"></a>

# stackific.mcp.protocol.ui\_host

Interactive UI Extension II: UI-to-Host Dialect, Registry & Security (§26.5–§26.9).

The runtime, *dynamic* half of the OPTIONAL Interactive User-Interface ("apps")
extension: the JSON-RPC 2.0 message dialect a rendered UI (running in its sandbox)
speaks with its host over a host-provided channel, the verbatim method/notification name
registry that dialect uses, and the normative security/consent model a host MUST enforce
around it. :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.ui` established *what* a UI is and how it is declared and
served; this module defines *how* it talks to the host once rendered, and how that
channel is kept safe.

The dialect is framed identically to core MCP (§3): every message is a JSON-RPC request,
response, or notification. It reuses a small subset of core method names verbatim
(``tools/call``, ``resources/read``, ``ping``, ``notifications/message``) and adds the
``ui/``-prefixed names. Its handshake carries its OWN protocol-version revision
(:data:`UI_DIALECT_PROTOCOL_VERSION`, ``"2026-01-26"``) — independent of the core
revision negotiated at ``server/discover``.

As with :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.ui`, rendering, sandboxing, CSP/permission enforcement,
running the channel runtime, and obtaining user consent are HOST responsibilities and
are NOT obligations of a server SDK (R-26.9-d). This module therefore models the dialect
declaratively — message validators, the registry, and host predicates/builders a host
implementation can consult — but never renders anything and takes no browser/UI-toolkit
dependency.

REUSE (never redefined here):

* the §26.1–§26.4 UI symbols — :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.ui`
  (:func:`~mcp.protocol.ui.host_should_reject_ui_originated_call`,
  :func:`~mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_permissions`,
  :func:`~mcp.protocol.ui.is_ui_content_security_policy`, …);
* the JSON-RPC framing — :mod:`stackific.mcp.jsonrpc.framing`
  (:func:`~mcp.jsonrpc.framing.classify_message`,
  :class:`~mcp.jsonrpc.framing.MalformedMessageError`);
* the §22 error model — :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.errors`
  (:data:`~mcp.protocol.errors.METHOD_NOT_FOUND_CODE`,
  :data:`~mcp.protocol.errors.INVALID_PARAMS_CODE`,
  :data:`~mcp.protocol.errors.INTERNAL_ERROR_CODE`,
  :func:`~mcp.protocol.errors.build_error_object`);
* the core logging notification method name — :mod:`stackific.mcp.protocol.logging`
  (:data:`~mcp.protocol.logging.LOGGING_MESSAGE_METHOD`);
* the §14 content-block shape — :func:`~mcp.types.content.is_valid_content_block`.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UiDisplayMode"></a>

#### UiDisplayMode

A display mode + a §14 ``ContentBlock`` array, as field types for the dialect models.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UI_DIALECT_PROTOCOL_VERSION"></a>

#### UI\_DIALECT\_PROTOCOL\_VERSION

The exact, case-sensitive protocol-version string carried in this dialect's
initialization handshake. It identifies the *message-dialect* revision and is
INDEPENDENT of the core protocol revision negotiated at ``server/discover``.
This is deliberately a distinct constant from any core-revision string: the two
revisions evolve separately, and conflating them is a conformance error.
(§26.5, R-26.5-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.is_ui_dialect_protocol_version"></a>

#### is\_ui\_dialect\_protocol\_version

```python
def is_ui_dialect_protocol_version(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is exactly :data:`UI_DIALECT_PROTOCOL_VERSION` —
matched byte-for-byte and case-sensitively. (R-26.5-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UI_DISPLAY_MODES"></a>

#### UI\_DISPLAY\_MODES

The three display modes a UI may run in / request. ``"inline"`` — embedded inline
within the host surface; ``"fullscreen"`` — occupying the whole host viewport;
``"pip"`` — a picture-in-picture / floating presentation. (§26.5.1, §26.5.3)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.is_ui_display_mode"></a>

#### is\_ui\_display\_mode

```python
def is_ui_display_mode(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is one of the exact display-mode enum strings,
matched case-sensitively. (§26.5.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UI_DIALECT_METHODS"></a>

#### UI\_DIALECT\_METHODS

The complete set of dialect method and notification names, reproduced VERBATIM and
case-sensitively. These are the only names a conforming dialect message may carry; a
name that is not byte-for-byte one of these is not part of the dialect.
``LOG_MESSAGE`` is the core logging method name reused verbatim. (§26.6, R-26.5-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UiDialectRegistryEntry"></a>

## UiDialectRegistryEntry Objects

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class UiDialectRegistryEntry()
```

One row of the §26.6 registry: the verbatim name, its kind, and its direction.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UiDialectRegistryEntry.name"></a>

#### name

The verbatim, case-sensitive method/notification name. (R-26.5-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UiDialectRegistryEntry.kind"></a>

#### kind

Whether the message is a ``"request"`` or a ``"notification"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UiDialectRegistryEntry.sender"></a>

#### sender

Which side originates the message (the §26.6 "Sender" column): one of
``"ui-to-host"``, ``"host-to-ui"``, ``"ui-or-host"``, ``"sandbox-to-host"``,
``"host-to-sandbox"``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.UI_DIALECT_REGISTRY"></a>

#### UI\_DIALECT\_REGISTRY

The complete §26.6 registry, in spec order: all 19 distinct names with their kind and
direction. The host validates a dialect message's ``method`` against this table
byte-for-byte. (§26.6, R-26.5-a; covers AC-42.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.is_ui_dialect_method_name"></a>

#### is\_ui\_dialect\_method\_name

```python
def is_ui_dialect_method_name(name: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``name`` is one of the verbatim dialect method/notification
names — matched byte-for-byte and case-sensitively, so ``"UI/Initialize"`` or
``"ui/Initialize"`` do NOT match. (§26.6, R-26.5-a; AC-42.1)

<a id="stackific.mcp.protocol.ui_host.ui_dialect_registry_entry"></a>

#### ui\_dialect\_registry\_entry

```python
def ui_dialect_registry_entry(name: str) -> UiDialectRegistryEntry | None
```

Return the §26.6 registry entry for ``name``, or ``None`` if not a dialect name.
