<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.Base64Str"></a>

#### Base64Str

A Base64 string field — the analogue of Zod ``z.string().refine(isValidBase64)``.

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.TextResourceContents"></a>

## TextResourceContents Objects

```python
class TextResourceContents(McpModel)
```

Text variant of resource contents (§14.5). Use only when the resource is
representable as text rather than binary data. (R-14.5-d, R-14.5-e)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.TextResourceContents.uri"></a>

#### uri

REQUIRED. URI identifying the resource. (R-14.5-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.TextResourceContents.mime_type"></a>

#### mime\_type

OPTIONAL. MIME type of the resource, if known. (R-14.5-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.TextResourceContents.text"></a>

#### text

REQUIRED. Textual content. (R-14.5-d)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.TextResourceContents.meta"></a>

#### meta

OPTIONAL. Implementation-specific metadata. (R-14.5-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.BlobResourceContents"></a>

## BlobResourceContents Objects

```python
class BlobResourceContents(McpModel)
```

Binary variant of resource contents (§14.5). ``blob`` is Base64-encoded raw bytes. (R-14.5-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.BlobResourceContents.uri"></a>

#### uri

REQUIRED. URI identifying the resource. (R-14.5-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.BlobResourceContents.mime_type"></a>

#### mime\_type

OPTIONAL. MIME type of the resource, if known. (R-14.5-b)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.BlobResourceContents.blob"></a>

#### blob

REQUIRED. Binary content encoded as Base64. (R-14.5-f)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.BlobResourceContents.meta"></a>

#### meta

OPTIONAL. Implementation-specific metadata. (R-14.5-c)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.ResourceContents"></a>

#### ResourceContents

The concrete contents of a resource: text or binary, selected by payload field. (§14.5)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.parse_resource_contents"></a>

#### parse\_resource\_contents

```python
def parse_resource_contents(
        value: object) -> TextResourceContents | BlobResourceContents
```

Parse a ``ResourceContents`` value, selecting the variant by payload field.

**Raises**:

- `ValidationError`: when the value carries both/neither payload, or is malformed.

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.is_valid_text_resource_contents"></a>

#### is\_valid\_text\_resource\_contents

```python
def is_valid_text_resource_contents(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``TextResourceContents`` (REQUIRED ``uri`` + ``text``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.is_valid_blob_resource_contents"></a>

#### is\_valid\_blob\_resource\_contents

```python
def is_valid_blob_resource_contents(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``BlobResourceContents`` (REQUIRED ``uri`` + Base64 ``blob``).

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.resource_contents.is_valid_resource_contents"></a>

#### is\_valid\_resource\_contents

```python
def is_valid_resource_contents(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` for valid ``ResourceContents`` — exactly one of ``text``/``blob`` (§14.5).

A value carrying BOTH is rejected. (R-14.5-g, R-14.5-h)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.role"></a>

# stackific.mcp.types.role

``Role`` — the two-value conversation-participant enumeration (§14.7).

Used by ``Annotations.audience`` and by prompt messages. The set is closed: only
``"user"`` and ``"assistant"`` are valid. (R-14.7-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.role.Role"></a>

#### Role

A conversation participant — the closed two-value set (§14.7, R-14.7-a). The Python
analogue of the TS ``RoleSchema`` (a Zod enum); used as a field type on model schemas.

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.role.ROLES"></a>

#### ROLES

The closed set of conversation roles. (§14.7, R-14.7-a)

<a id="stackific.mcp.types.role.is_role"></a>

#### is\_role

```python
def is_role(value: object) -> bool
```

Return ``True`` when ``value`` is a valid ``Role`` (``"user"`` or ``"assistant"``).

Roles are strings, so any non-string value (including unhashable ones such as
``list``/``dict``) is rejected before the set-membership test — mirroring the TS
``typeof value === 'string'`` guard.
